exam: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer - Associate

questions.question: A company is building a web-based AI application by using Amazon SageMaker. The application will provide the following capabilities and features: ML experimentation, training, a central model registry, model deployment, and model monitoring. The application must ensure secure and isolated use of training data during the ML lifecycle. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The company needs to use the central model registry to manage different versions of models in the application. Which action will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Create a separate Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) repository for each model.
options.B: Use Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) and unique tags for each model version.
options.C: Use the SageMaker Model Registry and model groups to catalog the models.
options.D: Use the SageMaker Model Registry and unique tags for each model version.

questions.explanation: C. Use the SageMaker Model Registry and model groups to catalog the models. SageMaker Model Registry is specifically designed for managing ML models within the SageMaker ecosystem. It provides built-in versioning and cataloging capabilities for ML models. Model groups in the Model Registry allow for logical organization of related models. It integrates seamlessly with other SageMaker components like training, deployment, and monitoring. This solution offers the least operational overhead as it's a native SageMaker feature designed for this exact purpose. It provides features like model approval workflows, which are crucial for managing models in production environments.

questions.question: A company is building a web-based AI application by using Amazon SageMaker. The application will provide the following capabilities and features: ML experimentation, training, a central model registry, model deployment, and model monitoring. The application must ensure secure and isolated use of training data during the ML lifecycle. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The company is experimenting with consecutive training jobs. How can the company MINIMIZE infrastructure startup times for these jobs?

options.A: Use Managed Spot Training.
options.B: Use SageMaker managed warm pools.
options.C: Use SageMaker Training Compiler.
options.D: Use the SageMaker distributed data parallelism (SMDDP) library.

questions.explanation: B, SageMaker managed warm pools. This feature is designed specifically to address the issue of minimizing infrastructure startup times for consecutive training jobs. Managed warm pools keep the underlying infrastructure for training jobs active for a specified period after a job completes. When the next training job starts, if it's within the warm pool period and has compatible settings, it can reuse this pre-provisioned infrastructure instead of having to provision new resources from scratch. This significantly reduces the time it takes for the job to start, as it bypasses the initial setup and provisioning steps. This is particularly useful for consecutive training jobs where you might be fine-tuning a model or running a series of experiments.

questions.question: A company is building a web-based AI application by using Amazon SageMaker. The application will provide the following capabilities and features: ML experimentation, training, a central model registry, model deployment, and model monitoring. The application must ensure secure and isolated use of training data during the ML lifecycle. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The company must implement a manual approval-based workflow to ensure that only approved models can be deployed to production endpoints. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Use SageMaker Experiments to facilitate the approval process during model registration.
options.B: Use SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking on the central model registry. Create tracking entities for the approval process.
options.C: Use SageMaker Model Monitor to evaluate the performance of the model and to manage the approval.
options.D: Use SageMaker Pipelines. When a model version is registered, use the AWS SDK to change the approval status to "Approved."

questions.explanation: D. Use SageMaker Pipelines. When a model version is registered, use the AWS SDK to change the approval status to "Approved." SageMaker Pipelines allows you to automate and manage the end-to-end machine learning (ML) workflow, including model training, validation, and deployment. You can introduce manual approval gates in the pipeline that will control when a model is approved for deployment. With SageMaker Pipelines, you can integrate approval workflows using custom actions, which ensures only approved models get deployed to production endpoints. The AWS SDK can be used to interact with the pipeline, allowing you to update the approval status of models when they are registered or after manual approval.

questions.question: A company is building a web-based AI application by using Amazon SageMaker. The application will provide the following capabilities and features: ML experimentation, training, a central model registry, model deployment, and model monitoring. The application must ensure secure and isolated use of training data during the ML lifecycle. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The company needs to run an on-demand workflow to monitor bias drift for models that are deployed to real-time endpoints from the application. Which action will meet this requirement?

options.A: Configure the application to invoke an AWS Lambda function that runs a SageMaker Clarify job.
options.B: Invoke an AWS Lambda function to pull the sagemaker-model-monitor-analyzer built-in SageMaker image.
options.C: Use AWS Glue Data Quality to monitor bias.
options.D: Use SageMaker notebooks to compare the bias.

questions.explanation: A. Configure the application to invoke an AWS Lambda function that runs a SageMaker Clarify job. This approach effectively meets the requirement for an on-demand workflow to monitor bias drift. Here’s why: SageMaker Clarify is the specialized service within Amazon SageMaker specifically designed to detect and monitor bias in machine learning models. It can analyze both pre-training data and post-deployment model predictions for various types of bias. AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that lets you run code without provisioning or managing servers. It's the perfect tool for creating an on-demand workflow because it can be triggered by an event, such as a user action in the application. The combination of these two services creates a powerful, scalable, and cost-effective solution. The application can trigger a Lambda function, which in turn initiates a SageMaker Clarify job to analyze the model's predictions for bias drift. This workflow is automated, repeatable, and doesn't require constant infrastructure management.

questions.question: A company stores historical data in .csv files in Amazon S3. Only some of the rows and columns in the .csv files are populated. The columns are not labeled. An ML engineer needs to prepare and store the data so that the company can use the data to train ML models. Select and order the correct steps from the following list to perform this task. Each step should be selected one time or not at all. (Select and order three.) • Create an Amazon SageMaker batch transform job for data cleaning and feature engineering. • Store the resulting data back in Amazon S3. • Use Amazon Athena to infer the schemas and available columns. • Use AWS Glue crawlers to infer the schemas and available columns. • Use AWS Glue DataBrew for data cleaning and feature engineering.
options: Create an Amazon SageMaker batch transform job for data cleaning and feature engineering.
Store the resulting data back in Amazon S3.
Use Amazon Athena to infer the schemas and available columns.
Use AWS Glue crawlers to infer the schemas and available columns.
Use AWS Glue DataBrew for data cleaning and feature engineering.
answer: Use AWS Glue crawlers to infer the schemas and available columns
Use AWS Glue DataBrew for data cleaning and feature engineering
Store the resulting data back in Amazon S3
questions.explanation: Step 1: Use AWS Glue crawlers to infer the schemas and available columns. AWS Glue Crawlers scan data in Amazon S3 to infer schema and structure, automatically creating metadata tables in the Glue Data Catalog. This step is ideal as the first step in a data pipeline when you're preparing raw data. Automatically discovers schemas, data types, and partitions. Step 2: Use AWS Glue DataBrew for data cleaning and feature engineering. AWS Glue DataBrew is a visual data preparation tool that lets users clean and transform data without writing code. After schema discovery, the next logical step is to transform and clean the data — removing nulls, standardizing formats, etc. Data cleaning, transformation, feature engineering. Preview transformations before applying them. Step 3: Store the resulting data back in Amazon S3. After cleaning and transforming the data with DataBrew (or any other tool), you typically store the refined dataset back to Amazon S3. Amazon S3 acts as a central data lake for storage, sharing, or further processing (e.g., using Athena or SageMaker).

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to use Amazon SageMaker Feature Store to create and manage features to train a model. Select and order the steps from the following list to create and use the features in Feature Store. Each step should be selected one time. (Select and order three.) • Access the store to build datasets for training. • Create a feature group. • Ingest the records.
options: Access the store to build datasets for training.
answer: Access the store to build datasets for training
questions.explanation: Step 1: Create a feature group. A Feature Group is like a schema for storing features. It defines the structure of the features you want to store, including: Feature names Data types Record identifiers Event timestamps Creating a feature group is the first step before you can store any data in the Feature Store. Think of this like defining a table in a relational database before inserting data into it. Step 2: Ingest the records. Once the Feature Group is created, you can ingest (insert) the actual data/records into it. This means populating the Feature Group with rows of data where each row represents a specific entity (e.g., a user, product, or session). Ingested records are stored in online and/or offline stores, depending on how the Feature Group is configured. Step 3: Access the store to build datasets for training. After data is ingested, you can retrieve the features from the Feature Store (usually the offline store) to build training datasets for machine learning models. This is typically done before launching training jobs in Amazon SageMaker.

questions.question: A company wants to host an ML model on Amazon SageMaker. An ML engineer is configuring a continuous integration and continuous delivery (Cl/CD) pipeline in AWS CodePipeline to deploy the model. The pipeline must run automatically when new training data for the model is uploaded to an Amazon S3 bucket. Select and order the pipeline's correct steps from the following list. Each step should be selected one time or not at all. (Select and order three.) • An S3 event notification invokes the pipeline when new data is uploaded. • S3 Lifecycle rule invokes the pipeline when new data is uploaded. • SageMaker retrains the model by using the data in the S3 bucket. • The pipeline deploys the model to a SageMaker endpoint. • The pipeline deploys the model to SageMaker Model Registry.
options: An S3 event notification invokes the pipeline when new data is uploaded.
S3 Lifecycle rule invokes the pipeline when new data is uploaded.
SageMaker retrains the model by using the data in the S3 bucket.
The pipeline deploys the model to a SageMaker endpoint.
The pipeline deploys the model to SageMaker Model Registry.
answer: An S3 event notification invokes the pipeline when new data is uploaded
SageMaker retrains the model by using the data in the S3 bucket
The pipeline deploys the model to a SageMaker endpoint
questions.explanation: Step 1: An S3 event notification invokes the pipeline when new data is uploaded. In AWS, an S3 event notification is often used to trigger a pipeline when new data is uploaded to a bucket. This is a common approach to automate ML workflows by detecting new training data and triggering retraining processes. Step 2: SageMaker retrains the model by using the data in the S3 bucket. Once the pipeline is triggered by the S3 event, SageMaker retrieves the new data from the S3 bucket and retrains the ML model. This is a key step in continuous model training workflows. Step 3: The pipeline deploys the model to a SageMaker endpoint. After retraining, the final step in an ML pipeline is often deployment. The trained model is deployed to a SageMaker endpoint so it can be used for inference in real-time applications.

questions.question: An ML engineer is building a generative AI application on Amazon Bedrock by using large language models (LLMs). Select the correct generative AI term from the following list for each description. Each term should be selected one time or not at all. (Select three.) • Embedding • Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) • Temperature • Token

answer.statement: Text representation of basic units of data processed by LLMs
High-dimensional vectors that contain the semantic meaning of text
Enrichment of information from additional data sources to improve a generated response

questions.explanation: 1. Text representation of basic units of data processed by LLMs Token. Tokens are the smallest units of text that LLMs process. A token can be a word, part of a word, or even punctuation depending on the tokenizer. LLMs (like GPT) break input text into tokens before performing predictions. 2. High-dimensional vectors that contain the semantic meaning of text Embedding. Embeddings are numerical representations of text in a high-dimensional space. These vectors preserve the semantic meaning, allowing LLMs or other models to understand the context and relationships between words or phrases. 3. Enrichment of information from additional data sources to improve a generated response Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) RAG is a technique where the model retrieves external documents or data (e.g., from a vector store or a knowledge base) before generating a response. This helps the model augment its output with factual or contextual information it might not have memorized.

questions.question: An ML engineer is working on an ML model to predict the prices of similarly sized homes. The model will base predictions on several features The ML engineer will use the following feature engineering techniques to estimate the prices of the homes: • Feature splitting • Logarithmic transformation • One-hot encoding • Standardized distribution Select the correct feature engineering techniques for the following list of features. Each feature engineering technique should be selected one time or not at all (Select three.)

answer.statement: Type_year (type of home and year the home was built)
Size of the building (square feet or square meters)

questions.explanation: 1. City (name) :One-hot encoding. The city name is a categorical variable (nominal data) that represents a set of distinct categories without any inherent order. One-hot encoding is a common technique for handling categorical variables where each category is converted into a binary (0 or 1) vector. This avoids assigning any numerical relationship between categories, which would be misleading in a model. Example: If we have three cities—New York, Los Angeles, and Chicago—one-hot encoding would transform them into: New York → [1, 0, 0] Los Angeles → [0, 1, 0] Chicago → [0, 0, 1] 2. Type_year (type of home and year the home was built) : Feature splitting. This feature contains two types of information: (1) the type of home and (2) the year it was built. Feature splitting means breaking this single column into two separate features: one for home type and one for year. Example: "Apartment_2000" → Apartment (categorical feature) and 2000 (numerical feature) This improves data representation and allows the model to process each type of information correctly. 3.Size of the building (square feet or square meters) : Logarithmic transformation. The size of a building is a continuous numerical variable that can have a wide range of values. Logarithmic transformation helps normalize skewed distributions where there are a few extremely large values. It reduces the impact of large outliers and makes the data more normally distributed, improving model performance. Example: Raw data: 500, 1000, 5000, 10000, 50000 After log transformation: 2.7, 3.0, 3.7, 4.0, 4.7 (values are compressed in a more manageable range).

questions.question: An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. Which AWS service or feature can aggregate the data from the various data sources?

options.A: Amazon EMR Spark jobs
options.B: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
options.C: Amazon DynamoDB
options.D: AWS Lake Formation

questions.explanation: Amazon EMR with Spark is an excellent choice for aggregating, processing, and transforming large datasets from multiple sources (e.g., Amazon S3 and on-premises MySQL database). Spark jobs can handle both structured and unstructured. While Lake Formation is great for managing data lakes, it doesn’t provide the ETL and data processing capabilities required to aggregate and transform datasets from multiple sources.

questions.question: An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. After the data is aggregated, the ML engineer must implement a solution to automatically detect anomalies in the data and to visualize the result. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon Athena to automatically detect the anomalies and to visualize the result.
options.B: Use Amazon Redshift Spectrum to automatically detect the anomalies. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the result.
options.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to automatically detect the anomalies and to visualize the result.
options.D: Use AWS Batch to automatically detect the anomalies. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the result.

questions.explanation: C. Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to automatically detect the anomalies and to visualize the result. SageMaker Data Wrangler is designed to simplify the data preparation and feature engineering process. It provides built-in capabilities to detect anomalies and data quality issues automatically. It supports seamless integration with data sources like Amazon S3 and on-premises databases via connectors, so it can handle your aggregated dataset. It also has built-in visualization tools to help you explore data distributions, correlations, and detect anomalies visually without needing separate tools. This fits well with the requirement to automatically detect anomalies and visualize results within the same workflow.

questions.question: An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. The training dataset includes categorical data and numerical data. The ML engineer must prepare the training dataset to maximize the accuracy of the model. Which action will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Use AWS Glue to transform the categorical data into numerical data.
options.B: Use AWS Glue to transform the numerical data into categorical data.
options.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to transform the categorical data into numerical data.
options.D: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to transform the numerical data into categorical data.

questions.explanation: C. Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to transform the categorical data into numerical data. Categorical data typically needs to be encoded into numerical formats (e.g., one-hot encoding, label encoding) so ML algorithms can process them effectively. SageMaker Data Wrangler provides a user-friendly, low-code environment to handle data transformations, including converting categorical features into numerical representations. It integrates seamlessly with the rest of the SageMaker ecosystem, helping reduce manual work and operational complexity. It supports visual interaction and automatic suggestions, which minimizes overhead compared to writing complex ETL scripts.

questions.question: An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. Before the ML engineer trains the model, the ML engineer must resolve the issue of the imbalanced data. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational effort?

options.A: Use Amazon Athena to identify patterns that contribute to the imbalance. Adjust the dataset accordingly.
options.B: Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic built-in algorithms to process the imbalanced dataset.
options.C: Use AWS Glue DataBrew built-in features to oversample the minority class.
options.D: Use the Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler balance data operation to oversample the minority class.

questions.explanation: D. Use the Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler's balance data operation to oversample the minority class. Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler is a purpose-built service for data preparation and feature engineering. It provides a visual interface and a variety of built-in transformations to help prepare data for machine learning. This approach offers the least operational effort for several reasons: Built-in functionality: Data Wrangler has a "balance data" operation that can be applied with a few clicks. This operation can automatically oversample the minority class or undersample the majority class, directly addressing the class imbalance issue. No-code/low-code environment: It's a managed service with a visual interface, so you don't need to write and maintain complex code to handle the data imbalance. This drastically reduces the time and effort required. Integrated with SageMaker: Data Wrangler is part of the SageMaker ecosystem, making it easy to integrate the data preparation steps directly into your ML workflow.

questions.question: An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model on AWS. The training dataset includes transaction logs, customer profiles, and tables from an on-premises MySQL database. The transaction logs and customer profiles are stored in Amazon S3. The dataset has a class imbalance that affects the learning of the model's algorithm. Additionally, many of the features have interdependencies. The algorithm is not capturing all the desired underlying patterns in the data. The ML engineer needs to use an Amazon SageMaker built-in algorithm to train the model. Which algorithm should the ML engineer use to meet this requirement?

options.A: LightGBM
options.B: Linear learner
options.C: К-means clustering
options.D: Neural Topic Model (NTM)

questions.explanation: A. LightGBM. LightGBM is a gradient boosting framework that excels at handling structured/tabular data like transaction logs and customer profiles. It can handle class imbalance better by adjusting parameters like is_unbalance or scale_pos_weight. It can model complex feature interdependencies and non-linear relationships effectively. It is designed for high accuracy and is a popular choice for fraud detection and similar classification problems.

questions.question: A company has deployed an XGBoost prediction model in production to predict if a customer is likely to cancel a subscription. The company uses Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor to detect deviations in the F1 score. During a baseline analysis of model quality, the company recorded a threshold for the F1 score. After several months of no change, the model's F1 score decreases significantly. What could be the reason for the reduced F1 score?

options.A: Concept drift occurred in the underlying customer data that was used for predictions.
options.B: The model was not sufficiently complex to capture all the patterns in the original baseline data.
options.C: The original baseline data had a data quality issue of missing values.
options.D: Incorrect ground truth labels were provided to Model Monitor during the calculation of the baseline.

questions.explanation: A. Concept drift occurred in the underlying customer data that was used for predictions. Concept drift means the statistical properties of the target variable, which the model is predicting, change over time. This causes the model, which was trained on past data, to become less accurate because the patterns it learned no longer represent the current data. Since the F1 score dropped significantly after months of stability, it strongly indicates that the input data distribution or the relationship between features and the target has changed.

questions.question: A company has a team of data scientists who use Amazon SageMaker notebook instances to test ML models. When the data scientists need new permissions, the company attaches the permissions to each individual role that was created during the creation of the SageMaker notebook instance. The company needs to centralize management of the team's permissions. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Create a single IAM role that has the necessary permissions. Attach the role to each notebook instance that the team uses.
options.B: Create a single IAM group. Add the data scientists to the group. Associate the group with each notebook instance that the team uses.
options.C: Create a single IAM user. Attach the AdministratorAccess AWS managed IAM policy to the user. Configure each notebook instance to use the IAM user.
options.D: Create a single IAM group. Add the data scientists to the group. Create an IAM role. Attach the AdministratorAccess AWS managed IAM policy to the role. Associate the role with the group. Associate the group with each notebook instance that the team uses.

questions.explanation: A. Create a single IAM role that has the necessary permissions. Attach the role to each notebook instance that the team uses. SageMaker notebook instances use IAM roles (not users or groups) to define permissions for the notebook instance. By creating a single IAM role with all the required permissions, you can attach this role to each notebook instance, centralizing permission management. This way, when permissions need to be updated, you only modify the single role rather than updating multiple individual roles. This approach aligns with AWS best practices of using IAM roles for service permissions.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to use an ML model to predict the price of apartments in a specific location. Which metric should the ML engineer use to evaluate the model's performance?

options.A: Accuracy
options.B: Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC)
options.C: F1 score
options.D: Mean absolute error (MAE)

questions.explanation: D. Mean absolute error (MAE) Predicting apartment prices is a regression problem because the target variable is continuous. Mean Absolute Error (MAE) measures the average magnitude of errors in predictions without considering their direction. It tells you how close the predicted values are to the actual prices on average. MAE is intuitive and widely used for regression model evaluation.

questions.question: An ML engineer has trained a neural network by using stochastic gradient descent (SGD). The neural network performs poorly on the test set. The values for training loss and validation loss remain high and show an oscillating pattern. The values decrease for a few epochs and then increase for a few epochs before repeating the same cycle. What should the ML engineer do to improve the training process?

options.A: Introduce early stopping.
options.B: Increase the size of the test set.
options.C: Increase the learning rate.
options.D: Decrease the learning rate.

questions.explanation: D. Decrease the learning rate . Decreasing the learning rate makes the optimizer take smaller, more stable steps toward the minimum, reducing oscillations and improving convergence.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to process thousands of existing CSV objects and new CSV objects that are uploaded. The CSV objects are stored in a central Amazon S3 bucket and have the same number of columns. One of the columns is a transaction date. The ML engineer must query the data based on the transaction date. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Use an Amazon Athena CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) statement to create a table based on the transaction date from data in the central S3 bucket. Query the objects from the table.
options.B: Create a new S3 bucket for processed data. Set up S3 replication from the central S3 bucket to the new S3 bucket. Use S3 Object Lambda to query the objects based on transaction date.
options.C: Create a new S3 bucket for processed data. Use AWS Glue for Apache Spark to create a job to query the CSV objects based on transaction date. Configure the job to store the results in the new S3 bucket. Query the objects from the new S3 bucket.
options.D: Create a new S3 bucket for processed data. Use Amazon Data Firehose to transfer the data from the central S3 bucket to the new S3 bucket. Configure Firehose to run an AWS Lambda function to query the data based on transaction date.

questions.explanation: A. Use an Amazon Athena CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) statement to create a table based on the transaction date from data in the central S3 bucket. Query the objects from the table. Amazon Athena allows you to directly query data stored in S3 using standard SQL without needing to move or transform the data first. Using CTAS lets you create a new table with partitions or filtered data (e.g., by transaction date), improving query performance. Athena is serverless, so there’s no infrastructure to manage, minimizing operational overhead. Athena can work directly on new CSV objects as they arrive in the bucket without needing to copy or process them separately.

questions.question: A company has a large, unstructured dataset. The dataset includes many duplicate records across several key attributes. Which solution on AWS will detect duplicates in the dataset with the LEAST code development?

options.A: Use Amazon Mechanical Turk jobs to detect duplicates.
options.B: Use Amazon QuickSight ML Insights to build a custom deduplication model.
options.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to pre-process and detect duplicates.
options.D: Use the AWS Glue FindMatches transform to detect duplicates.

questions.explanation: D. Use the AWS Glue FindMatches transform to detect duplicates. AWS Glue FindMatches is a built-in machine learning transform specifically designed to identify duplicate or matching records in datasets. It requires minimal code, as it automates the process of entity resolution and duplicate detection. It integrates seamlessly with AWS Glue ETL jobs, making it easy to incorporate into data pipelines. Suitable for unstructured and semi-structured data with complex matching criteria across several attributes.

questions.question: A company needs to run a batch data-processing job on Amazon EC2 instances. The job will run during the weekend and will take 90 minutes to finish running. The processing can handle interruptions. The company will run the job every weekend for the next 6 months. Which EC2 instance purchasing option will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

options.A: Spot Instances
options.B: Reserved Instances
options.C: On-Demand Instances
options.D: Dedicated Instances

questions.explanation: A. Spot Instances Spot Instances offer the lowest price compared to On-Demand or Reserved Instances by using spare AWS capacity.

questions.question: An ML engineer has an Amazon Comprehend custom model in Account A in the us-east-1 Region. The ML engineer needs to copy the model to Account В in the same Region. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST development effort?

options.A: Use Amazon S3 to make a copy of the model. Transfer the copy to Account B.
options.B: Create a resource-based IAM policy. Use the Amazon Comprehend ImportModel API operation to copy the model to Account B.
options.C: Use AWS DataSync to replicate the model from Account A to Account B.
options.D: Create an AWS Site-to-Site VPN connection between Account A and Account В to transfer the model.

questions.explanation: B. Create a resource-based IAM policy. Use the Amazon Comprehend ImportModel API operation to copy the model to Account B. Amazon Comprehend supports model sharing and importing/exporting between accounts using the ImportModel API. You create a resource-based policy on the model in Account A that grants permission to Account B. Then, in Account B, you use the ImportModel API to import the shared model directly. This process is straightforward and designed for this exact use case, requiring minimal development effort.

questions.question: An ML engineer is training a simple neural network model. The ML engineer tracks the performance of the model over time on a validation dataset. The model's performance improves substantially at first and then degrades after a specific number of epochs. Which solutions will mitigate this problem? (Choose two.)

options.A: Enable early stopping on the model.
options.B: Increase dropout in the layers.
options.C: Increase the number of layers.
options.D: Increase the number of neurons.
options.E: Investigate and reduce the sources of model bias.

questions.explanation: A. Enable early stopping on the model. Early stopping monitors validation performance during training and stops training when the validation loss stops improving, preventing overfitting. B. Increase dropout in the layers. Dropout is a regularization technique that randomly disables neurons during training, reducing overfitting and improving generalization.

questions.question: A company has a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) application that uses a vector database to store embeddings of documents. The company must migrate the application to AWS and must implement a solution that provides semantic search of text files. The company has already migrated the text repository to an Amazon S3 bucket. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use an AWS Batch job to process the files and generate embeddings. Use AWS Glue to store the embeddings. Use SQL queries to perform the semantic searches.
options.B: Use a custom Amazon SageMaker notebook to run a custom script to generate embeddings. Use SageMaker Feature Store to store the embeddings. Use SQL queries to perform the semantic searches.
options.C: Use the Amazon Kendra S3 connector to ingest the documents from the S3 bucket into Amazon Kendra. Query Amazon Kendra to perform the semantic searches.
options.D: Use an Amazon Textract asynchronous job to ingest the documents from the S3 bucket. Query Amazon Textract to perform the semantic searches.

questions.explanation: C. Use the Amazon Kendra S3 connector to ingest the documents from the S3 bucket into Amazon Kendra. Query Amazon Kendra to perform the semantic searches. Amazon Kendra is a fully managed intelligent search service that supports semantic search out of the box. The S3 connector lets you easily ingest documents stored in S3 into Kendra without custom code. Kendra supports natural language queries and provides high-quality semantic search results, fitting perfectly with RAG use cases. It reduces operational overhead because it handles embedding generation, indexing, and search internally.

questions.question: A company uses Amazon Athena to query a dataset in Amazon S3. The dataset has a target variable that the company wants to predict. The company needs to use the dataset in a solution to determine if a model can predict the target variable. Which solution will provide this information with the LEAST development effort?

options.A: Create a new model by using Amazon SageMaker Autopilot. Report the model's achieved performance.
options.B: Implement custom scripts to perform data pre-processing, multiple linear regression, and performance evaluation. Run the scripts on Amazon EC2 instances.
options.C: Configure Amazon Macie to analyze the dataset and to create a model. Report the model's achieved performance.
options.D: Select a model from Amazon Bedrock. Tune the model with the data. Report the model's achieved performance.

questions.explanation: A. Create a new model by using Amazon SageMaker Autopilot. Report the model's achieved performance. Amazon SageMaker Autopilot automates the entire machine learning workflow: data preprocessing, feature engineering, model selection, training, tuning, and evaluation. It requires minimal code and manual intervention, making it the fastest way to build and assess a predictive model from a dataset. It directly provides model performance metrics, allowing easy determination of how well the model predicts the target variable.

questions.question: A company wants to predict the success of advertising campaigns by considering the color scheme of each advertisement. An ML engineer is preparing data for a neural network model. The dataset includes color information as categorical data. Which technique for feature engineering should the ML engineer use for the model?

options.A: Apply label encoding to the color categories. Automatically assign each color a unique integer.
options.B: Implement padding to ensure that all color feature vectors have the same length.
options.C: Perform dimensionality reduction on the color categories.
options.D: One-hot encode the color categories to transform the color scheme feature into a binary matrix.

questions.explanation: D. One-hot encode the color categories to transform the color scheme feature into a binary matrix. Neural networks perform better when categorical variables are represented as one-hot encoded vectors rather than integers. One-hot encoding avoids implying any ordinal relationship between categories, which label encoding (assigning unique integers) can mistakenly introduce. It converts each color category into a separate binary feature, allowing the network to learn weights for each color independently.

questions.question: A company uses a hybrid cloud environment. A model that is deployed on premises uses data in Amazon 53 to provide customers with a live conversational engine. The model is using sensitive data. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to identify and remove the sensitive data. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Deploy the model on Amazon SageMaker. Create a set of AWS Lambda functions to identify and remove the sensitive data.
options.B: Deploy the model on an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) cluster that uses AWS Fargate. Create an AWS Batch job to identify and remove the sensitive data.
options.C: Use Amazon Macie to identify the sensitive data. Create a set of AWS Lambda functions to remove the sensitive data.
options.D: Use Amazon Comprehend to identify the sensitive data. Launch Amazon EC2 instances to remove the sensitive data.

questions.explanation: C. Use Amazon Macie to identify the sensitive data. Create a set of AWS Lambda functions to remove the sensitive data. Amazon Macie is a fully managed service designed specifically to automatically discover, classify, and protect sensitive data in AWS data stores like Amazon S3. Macie requires minimal setup and operational effort to scan S3 data for sensitive information such as PII. Using AWS Lambda functions to remove or redact sensitive data enables a serverless, event-driven, and scalable approach with very low operational overhead. This approach leverages managed services and serverless compute, minimizing infrastructure management.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to create data ingestion pipelines and ML model deployment pipelines on AWS. All the raw data is stored in Amazon S3 buckets. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon Data Firehose to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic to create the model deployment pipelines.
options.B: Use AWS Glue to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic to create the model deployment pipelines.
options.C: Use Amazon Redshift ML to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic to create the model deployment pipelines.
options.D: Use Amazon Athena to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use an Amazon SageMaker notebook to create the model deployment pipelines.

questions.explanation: B. Use AWS Glue to create the data ingestion pipelines. Use Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic to create the model deployment pipelines. AWS Glue is a fully managed ETL service designed for building scalable, serverless data ingestion and transformation pipelines, especially from S3 and other sources. It can crawl, catalog, clean, and transform raw data efficiently, making it ideal for ingestion pipelines. Amazon SageMaker Studio Classic provides an integrated environment for developing, training, and deploying ML models, supporting end-to-end ML pipelines. This combination addresses both ingestion and deployment needs using managed services with minimal operational overhead.

questions.question: A company that has hundreds of data scientists is using Amazon SageMaker to create ML models. The models are in model groups in the SageMaker Model Registry. The data scientists are grouped into three categories: computer vision, natural language processing (NLP), and speech recognition. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to organize the existing models into these groups to improve model discoverability at scale. The solution must not affect the integrity of the model artifacts and their existing groupings. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create a custom tag for each of the three categories. Add the tags to the model packages in the SageMaker Model Registry.
options.B: Create a model group for each category. Move the existing models into these category model groups.
options.C: Use SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking to automatically identify and tag which model groups should contain the models.
options.D: Create a Model Registry collection for each of the three categories. Move the existing model groups into the collections.

questions.explanation: create a Model Registry collection for each of the three categories and move the existing model groups into the collections. SageMaker Model Registry collections are a feature designed to provide a higher level of organization and discoverability for machine learning models. They allow you to group multiple model groups into logical categories. Discoverability at Scale: By creating a collection for each of the three categories, you create a central, high- level view that makes it easy for users to browse and find models related to a specific category (e.g., "Customer Segmentation," "Fraud Detection," or "Computer Vision"). Preserves Integrity and Grouping: The prompt specifies that the solution must not affect the integrity of the model artifacts or their existing groupings. The Model Registry collection feature is perfect for this because it allows you to group existing model groups without changing the models within those groups. The model artifacts and their versions remain exactly where they are, preserving their integrity.

questions.question: A company runs an Amazon SageMaker domain in a public subnet of a newly created VPC. The network is configured properly, and ML engineers can access the SageMaker domain. Recently, the company discovered suspicious traffic to the domain from a specific IP address. The company needs to block traffic from the specific IP address. Which update to the network configuration will meet this requirement?

options.A: Create a security group inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the security group to the domain.
options.B: Create a network ACL inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the rule to the default network Ad for the subnet where the domain is located.
options.C: Create a shadow variant for the domain. Configure SageMaker Inference Recommender to send traffic from the specific IP address to the shadow endpoint.
options.D: Create a VPC route table to deny inbound traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the route table to the domain.

questions.explanation: B. Create a network ACL inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the rule to the network ACL for the subnet where the domain is located. Security groups are stateful and only support allow rules, not deny rules. So you cannot explicitly deny traffic from a security group inbound rule. Network ACLs (NACLs) are stateless and support both allow and deny rules, making them suitable for explicitly blocking specific IP addresses. Applying a deny rule for the suspicious IP address at the subnet's network ACL level effectively blocks the traffic before it reaches the SageMaker domain. Route tables control traffic routing but cannot be used to block inbound IP addresses.

questions.question: A company is gathering audio, video, and text data in various languages. The company needs to use a large language model (LLM) to summarize the gathered data that is in Spanish. Which solution will meet these requirements in the LEAST amount of time?

options.A: Train and deploy a model in Amazon SageMaker to convert the data into English text. Train and deploy an LLM in SageMaker to summarize the text.
options.B: Use Amazon Transcribe and Amazon Translate to convert the data into English text. Use Amazon Bedrock with the Jurassic model to summarize the text.
options.C: Use Amazon Rekognition and Amazon Translate to convert the data into English text. Use Amazon Bedrock with the Anthropic Claude model to summarize the text.
options.D: Use Amazon Comprehend and Amazon Translate to convert the data into English text. Use Amazon Bedrock with the Stable Diffusion model to summarize the text.

questions.explanation: B. Use Amazon Transcribe and Amazon Translate to convert the data into English text. Use Amazon Bedrock with the Jurassic model to summarize the text. Amazon Transcribe can convert audio and video speech into text. Amazon Translate can translate Spanish text into English. Amazon Bedrock provides access to foundation LLMs like Jurassic, Anthropic, and others without managing infrastructure. Using these managed services together minimizes development and deployment time, fulfilling the requirement in the least amount of time. Jurassic models on Bedrock are well-suited for summarization tasks.

questions.question: A financial company receives a high volume of real-time market data streams from an external provider. The streams consist of thousands of JSON records every second. The company needs to implement a scalable solution on AWS to identify anomalous data points. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Ingest real-time data into Amazon Kinesis data streams. Use the built-in RANDOM_CUT_FOREST function in Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink to process the data streams and to detect data anomalies.
options.B: Ingest real-time data into Amazon Kinesis data streams. Deploy an Amazon SageMaker endpoint for real-time outlier detection. Create an AWS Lambda function to detect anomalies. Use the data streams to invoke the Lambda function.
options.C: Ingest real-time data into Apache Kafka on Amazon EC2 instances. Deploy an Amazon SageMaker endpoint for real-time outlier detection. Create an AWS Lambda function to detect anomalies. Use the data streams to invoke the Lambda function.
options.D: Send real-time data to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO queue. Create an AWS Lambda function to consume the queue messages. Program the Lambda function to start an AWS Glue extract, transform, and load (ETL) job for batch processing and anomaly detection.

questions.explanation: A. Ingest real-time data into Amazon Kinesis data streams. Use the built-in RANDOM_CUT_FOREST function in Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink to process the data streams and to detect data anomalies. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams is a fully managed service designed for real-time data ingestion at high scale. Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink integrates natively with Kinesis and provides a built-in RANDOM_CUT_FOREST (RCF) algorithm for anomaly detection on streaming data. This solution is serverless and fully managed, minimizing operational overhead. No need to manage infrastructure, deploy models manually, or coordinate multiple services.

questions.question: A company has a large collection of chat recordings from customer interactions after a product release. An ML engineer needs to create an ML model to analyze the chat data. The ML engineer needs to determine the success of the product by reviewing customer sentiments about the product. Which action should the ML engineer take to complete the evaluation in the LEAST amount of time?

options.A: Use Amazon Rekognition to analyze sentiments of the chat conversations.
options.B: Train a Naive Bayes classifier to analyze sentiments of the chat conversations.
options.C: Use Amazon Comprehend to analyze sentiments of the chat conversations.
options.D: Use random forests to classify sentiments of the chat conversations.

questions.explanation: C. Use Amazon Comprehend to analyze sentiments of the chat conversations. Amazon Comprehend is a fully managed natural language processing (NLP) service that provides built-in sentiment analysis. It requires no model training or setup, so you can quickly analyze large volumes of chat data. It supports multiple languages and can extract sentiment at the document or sentence level. This approach drastically reduces development time compared to training custom models.

questions.question: A company has a conversational AI assistant that sends requests through Amazon Bedrock to an Anthropic Claude large language model (LLM). Users report that when they ask similar questions multiple times, they sometimes receive different answers. An ML engineer needs to improve the responses to be more consistent and less random. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Increase the temperature parameter and the top_k parameter.
options.B: Increase the temperature parameter. Decrease the top_k parameter.
options.C: Decrease the temperature parameter. Increase the top_k parameter.
options.D: Decrease the temperature parameter and the top_k parameter.

questions.explanation: D. Decrease the temperature parameter and the top_k parameter. The temperature parameter controls randomness in the output. Lower temperature values (close to 0) make the model more deterministic and focused, reducing variability in responses. Higher temperature increases randomness and creativity. The top_k parameter limits the number of highest probability tokens considered at each step. Lower top_k reduces the token sampling space, making outputs more focused and consistent. Higher top_k increases diversity but also randomness.

questions.question: A company is using ML to predict the presence of a specific weed in a farmer's field. The company is using the Amazon SageMaker linear learner built-in algorithm with a value of multiclass_dassifier for the predictorjype hyperparameter. What should the company do to MINIMIZE false positives?

options.A: Set the value of the weight decay hyperparameter to zero.
options.B: Increase the number of training epochs.
options.C: Increase the value of the target_precision hyperparameter.
options.D: Change the value of the predictorjype hyperparameter to regressor.

questions.explanation: C. Increase the value of the target_precision hyperparameter. The target_precision hyperparameter adjusts the model’s threshold to prioritize precision over recall. Increasing target_precision will make the model more conservative in classifying positive cases, thus reducing false positives (incorrectly labeling negatives as positives). This adjustment helps balance precision and recall depending on business needs, especially when false positives are costly.

questions.question: A company has implemented a data ingestion pipeline for sales transactions from its ecommerce website. The company uses Amazon Data Firehose to ingest data into Amazon OpenSearch Service. The buffer interval of the Firehose stream is set for 60 seconds. An OpenSearch linear model generates real-time sales forecasts based on the data and presents the data in an OpenSearch dashboard. The company needs to optimize the data ingestion pipeline to support sub-second latency for the real-time dashboard. Which change to the architecture will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use zero buffering in the Firehose stream. Tune the batch size that is used in the PutRecordBatch operation.
options.B: Replace the Firehose stream with an AWS DataSync task. Configure the task with enhanced fan-out consumers.
options.C: Increase the buffer interval of the Firehose stream from 60 seconds to 120 seconds.
options.D: Replace the Firehose stream with an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue.

questions.explanation: A. Use zero buffering in the Firehose stream. Tune the batch size that is used in the PutRecordBatch operation. The current 60-second buffer interval causes Firehose to batch data before sending it to OpenSearch, resulting in latency. Setting buffer interval to zero (or the minimum allowed) reduces this delay, enabling near real-time ingestion. Tuning batch size optimizes throughput while minimizing latency. This approach keeps Firehose, which is designed for scalable strea

questions.question: A company has trained an ML model in Amazon SageMaker. The company needs to host the model to provide inferences in a production environment. The model must be highly available and must respond with minimum latency. The size of each request will be between 1 KB and 3 MB. The model will receive unpredictable bursts of requests during the day. The inferences must adapt proportionally to the changes in demand. How should the company deploy the model into production to meet these requirements?

options.A: Create a SageMaker real-time inference endpoint. Configure auto scaling. Configure the endpoint to present the existing model.
options.B: Deploy the model on an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) cluster. Use ECS scheduled scaling that is based on the CPU of the ECS cluster.
options.C: Install SageMaker Operator on an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster. Deploy the model in Amazon EKS. Set horizontal pod auto scaling to scale replicas based on the memory metric.
options.D: Use Spot Instances with a Spot Fleet behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB) for inferences. Use the ALBRequestCountPerTarget metric as the metric for auto scaling.

questions.explanation: A. Create a SageMaker real-time inference endpoint. Configure auto scaling. Configure the endpoint to present the existing model. SageMaker real-time endpoints are fully managed, optimized for low-latency, high-availability inference. They support automatic scaling (with Application Auto Scaling) to handle unpredictable bursts of traffic by scaling the number of instances up or down based on demand. SageMaker endpoints can handle request sizes between 1 KB and 3 MB efficiently. Minimal operational overhead compared to self-managed container or Kubernetes solutions. Built-in integration with load balancing and health checks ensures high availability.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to use an Amazon EMR cluster to process large volumes of data in batches. Any data loss is unacceptable. Which instance purchasing option will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

options.A: Run the primary node, core nodes, and task nodes on On-Demand Instances.
options.B: Run the primary node, core nodes, and task nodes on Spot Instances.
options.C: Run the primary node on an On-Demand Instance. Run the core nodes and task nodes on Spot Instances.
options.D: Run the primary node and core nodes on On-Demand Instances. Run the task nodes on Spot Instances.

questions.explanation: D. Run the primary node and core nodes on On-Demand Instances. Run the task nodes on Spot Instances. Primary node (also called master node) manages the cluster and must be highly available and reliable, so it should always run on On-Demand instances. Core nodes manage HDFS storage and processing. Since data is stored on core nodes, running them on On- Demand instances prevents data loss. Task nodes are used only for processing and do not store data persistently, so running them on Spot Instances reduces cost without risking data loss. This setup provides cost savings from Spot Instances where data loss is acceptable (task nodes), while maintaining data durability and availability on On-Demand instances for master and core nodes.

questions.question: A company wants to improve the sustainability of its ML operations. Which actions will reduce the energy usage and computational resources that are associated with the company's training jobs? (Choose two.)

options.A: Use Amazon SageMaker Debugger to stop training jobs when non-converging conditions are detected.
options.B: Use Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth for data labeling.
options.C: Deploy models by using AWS Lambda functions.
options.D: Use AWS Trainium instances for training.
options.E: Use PyTorch or TensorFlow with the distributed training option.

questions.explanation: A. Use Amazon SageMaker Debugger to stop training jobs when non-converging conditions are detected. Stops wasted compute when a job is failing to learn (or has already converged), directly saving energy and resources. D. Use AWS Trainium instances for training. Trainium chips are purpose-built for efficient model training and typically deliver better performance-per- watt than general-purpose instances, reducing energy use for the same workload.

questions.question: A company is planning to create several ML prediction models. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. The entire dataset is more than 5 ТВ in size and consists of CSV, JSON, Apache Parquet, and simple text files. The data must be processed in several consecutive steps. The steps include complex manipulations that can take hours to finish running. Some of the processing involves natural language processing (NLP) transformations. The entire process must be automated. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Process data at each step by using Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler. Automate the process by using Data Wrangler jobs.
options.B: Use Amazon SageMaker notebooks for each data processing step. Automate the process by using Amazon EventBridge.
options.C: Process data at each step by using AWS Lambda functions. Automate the process by using AWS Step Functions and Amazon EventBridge.
options.D: Use Amazon SageMaker Pipelines to create a pipeline of data processing steps. Automate the pipeline by using Amazon EventBridge.

questions.explanation: D. Use Amazon SageMaker Pipelines to create a pipeline of data processing steps. Automate the pipeline by using Amazon EventBridge. The dataset is very large (5+ TB) and involves complex, multi-step processing including NLP transformations that can take hours. Amazon SageMaker Pipelines is designed for building and automating end-to-end ML workflows, including multiple data processing, training, and evaluation steps. Pipelines support orchestration of long-running and complex steps with full automation and monitoring. Integration with Amazon EventBridge enables triggering and scheduling pipelines. This approach handles large-scale processing efficiently and is built for ML workflows.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to use AWS CloudFormation to create an ML model that an Amazon SageMaker endpoint will host. Which resource should the ML engineer declare in the CloudFormation template to meet this requirement?

options.A: AWS::SageMaker::Model
options.B: AWS::SageMaker::Endpoint
options.C: AWS::SageMaker::NotebookInstance
options.D: AWS::SageMaker::Pipeline

questions.explanation: A. AWS::SageMaker::Model In AWS CloudFormation, to host an ML model on a SageMaker endpoint, you first need to define the model itself using the AWS::SageMaker::Model resource. This resource specifies: The location of the model artifacts in Amazon S3 The container image for inference IAM execution roles

questions.question: An advertising company uses AWS Lake Formation to manage a data lake. The data lake contains structured data and unstructured data. The company's ML engineers are assigned to specific advertisement campaigns. The ML engineers must interact with the data through Amazon Athena and by browsing the data directly in an Amazon S3 bucket. The ML engineers must have access to only the resources that are specific to their assigned advertisement campaigns. Which solution will meet these requirements in the MOST operationally efficient way?

options.A: Configure IAM policies on an AWS Glue Data Catalog to restrict access to Athena based on the ML engineers' campaigns.
options.B: Store users and campaign information in an Amazon DynamoDB table. Configure DynamoDB Streams to invoke an AWS Lambda function to update S3 bucket policies.
options.C: Use Lake Formation to authorize AWS Glue to access the S3 bucket. Configure Lake Formation tags to map ML engineers to their campaigns.
options.D: Configure S3 bucket policies to restrict access to the S3 bucket based on the ML engineers' campaigns.

questions.explanation: C. Use Lake Formation to authorize AWS Glue to access the S3 bucket. Configure Lake Formation tags to map ML engineers to their campaigns. AWS Lake Formation provides fine-grained access control on both the AWS Glue Data Catalog and the underlying S3 data. Lake Formation tag-based access control lets you assign tags to data resources (like tables or files) and then grant permissions to users/groups based on those tags. This allows ML engineers to access only data relevant to their specific campaigns both via Amazon Athena (which uses the Glue Data Catalog) and by direct S3 access (through Lake Formation permissions). It centralizes and automates access control management, reducing operational overhead.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to use data with Amazon SageMaker Canvas to train an ML model. The data is stored in Amazon S3 and is complex in structure. The ML engineer must use a file format that minimizes processing time for the data. Which file format will meet these requirements?

options.A: CSV files compressed with Snappy
options.B: JSON objects in JSONL format
options.C: JSON files compressed with gzip
options.D: Apache Parquet files

questions.explanation: D. Apache Parquet files Apache Parquet is a columnar storage file format optimized for analytical queries. It supports efficient compression and encoding schemes that reduce file size and speed up data processing. Parquet is especially suited for complex and nested data structures. SageMaker Canvas supports Parquet, and it minimizes the I/O and CPU required to read the data, reducing processing time.

questions.question: An ML engineer is evaluating several ML models and must choose one model to use in production. The cost of false negative predictions by the models is much higher than the cost of false positive predictions. Which metric finding should the ML engineer prioritize the MOST when choosing the model?

options.A: Low precision
options.B: High precision
options.C: Low recall
options.D: High recall

questions.explanation: D. High recall Recall measures the ability of the model to correctly identify all positive cases (i.e., minimize false negatives). Since false negatives are costly, the model should catch as many positive cases as possible, which means high recall. Precision relates to false positives, which are less costly here, so precision is less critical. Low recall means more false negatives, which is undesirable.

questions.question: A company has trained and deployed an ML model by using Amazon SageMaker. The company needs to implement a solution to record and monitor all the API call events for the SageMaker endpoint. The solution also must provide a notification when the number of API call events breaches a threshold. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use SageMaker Debugger to track the inferences and to report metrics. Create a custom rule to provide a notification when the threshold is breached.
options.B: Use SageMaker Debugger to track the inferences and to report metrics. Use the tensor_variance built-in rule to provide a notification when the threshold is breached.
options.C: Log all the endpoint invocation API events by using AWS CloudTrail. Use an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard for monitoring. Set up a CloudWatch alarm to provide notification when the threshold is breached.
options.D: Add the Invocations metric to an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard for monitoring. Set up a CloudWatch alarm to provide notification when the threshold is breached.

questions.explanation: AWS CloudTrail logs all API call events, including SageMaker endpoint invocations. This satisfies the requirement to record all API call events. You can then use Amazon CloudWatch to monitor these logs and create a dashboard. Setting up a CloudWatch alarm allows notifications when API call counts breach a threshold.

questions.question: A company has AWS Glue data processing jobs that are orchestrated by an AWS Glue workflow. The AWS Glue jobs can run on a schedule or can be launched manually. The company is developing pipelines in Amazon SageMaker Pipelines for ML model development. The pipelines will use the output of the AWS Glue jobs during the data processing phase of model development. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution that integrates the AWS Glue jobs with the pipelines. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Use AWS Step Functions for orchestration of the pipelines and the AWS Glue jobs.
options.B: Use processing steps in SageMaker Pipelines. Configure inputs that point to the Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the AWS Glue jobs.
options.C: Use Callback steps in SageMaker Pipelines to start the AWS Glue workflow and to stop the pipelines until the AWS Glue jobs finish running.
options.D: Use Amazon EventBridge to invoke the pipelines and the AWS Glue jobs in the desired order.

questions.explanation: C. Use Callback steps in SageMaker Pipelines to start the AWS Glue workflow and to stop the pipelines until the AWS Glue jobs finish running. Callback steps in SageMaker Pipelines enable asynchronous integration with external workflows or jobs. You can start the AWS Glue workflow from a callback step and have the SageMaker Pipeline wait until Glue jobs complete. This approach provides tight integration and orchestration within SageMaker Pipelines itself, minimizing external orchestration tools and manual intervention. It avoids managing separate orchestration frameworks like Step Functions or EventBridge for this use case.

questions.question: A company is using an Amazon Redshift database as its single data source. Some of the data is sensitive. A data scientist needs to use some of the sensitive data from the database. An ML engineer must give the data scientist access to the data without transforming the source data and without storing anonymized data in the database. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.A: Configure dynamic data masking policies to control how sensitive data is shared with the data scientist at query time.
options.B: Create a materialized view with masking logic on top of the database. Grant the necessary read permissions to the data scientist.
options.C: Unload the Amazon Redshift data to Amazon S3. Use Amazon Athena to create schema-on-read with masking logic. Share the view with the data scientist.
options.D: Unload the Amazon Redshift data to Amazon S3. Create an AWS Glue job to anonymize the data. Share the dataset with the data scientist.

questions.explanation: A. Configure dynamic data masking policies to control how sensitive data is shared with the data scientist at query time. Dynamic data masking in Amazon Redshift lets you mask sensitive data in query results at runtime without changing or duplicating the underlying data. This approach does not require transforming or storing anonymized data, meeting the requirements directly. It minimizes implementation effort because you simply apply masking policies on the existing tables and grant the data scientist controlled access. The data scientist can query the data normally via Redshift but sees masked values for sensitive fields.

questions.question: An ML engineer is using a training job to fine-tune a deep learning model in Amazon SageMaker Studio. The ML engineer previously used the same pre-trained model with a similar dataset. The ML engineer expects vanishing gradient, underutilized GPU, and overfitting problems. The ML engineer needs to implement a solution to detect these issues and to react in predefined ways when the issues occur. The solution also must provide comprehensive real-time metrics during the training. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Use TensorBoard to monitor the training job. Publish the findings to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic. Create an AWS Lambda function to consume the findings and to initiate the predefined actions.
options.B: Use Amazon CloudWatch default metrics to gain insights about the training job. Use the metrics to invoke an AWS Lambda function to initiate the predefined actions.
options.C: Expand the metrics in Amazon CloudWatch to include the gradients in each training step. Use the metrics to invoke an AWS Lambda function to initiate the predefined actions.
options.D: Use SageMaker Debugger built-in rules to monitor the training job. Configure the rules to initiate the predefined actions.

questions.explanation: D. Use SageMaker Debugger built-in rules to monitor the training job. Configure the rules to initiate the predefined actions. SageMaker Debugger automatically captures real-time training metrics, including gradients, losses, and resource utilization. It provides built-in rules to detect common training issues such as: Vanishing or exploding gradients Underutilized GPU resources Overfitting Debugger can be configured to trigger actions (such as notifications or job stops) when rules are breached. This requires minimal setup compared to building custom monitoring pipelines. Provides a comprehensive view of training health and automates responses with low operational overhead.

questions.question: A credit card company has a fraud detection model in production on an Amazon SageMaker endpoint. The company develops a new version of the model. The company needs to assess the new model's performance by using live data and without affecting production end users. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Set up SageMaker Debugger and create a custom rule.
options.B: Set up blue/green deployments with all-at-once traffic shifting.
options.C: Set up blue/green deployments with canary traffic shifting.
options.D: Set up shadow testing with a shadow variant of the new model.

questions.explanation: D. Set up shadow testing with a shadow variant of the new model. Shadow testing (also called shadow deployment) sends live production traffic to both the current production model and the new model. The new model processes the same inputs but its predictions are not returned to end users, so production traffic and user experience remain unaffected. This allows the team to evaluate the new model’s performance on real, live data before switching it into production. It is a safe way to validate models without impacting customers.

questions.question: A company stores time-series data about user clicks in an Amazon S3 bucket. The raw data consists of millions of rows of user activity every day. ML engineers access the data to develop their ML models. The ML engineers need to generate daily reports and analyze click trends over the past 3 days by using Amazon Athena. The company must retain the data for 30 days before archiving the data. Which solution will provide the HIGHEST performance for data retrieval?

options.A: Keep all the time-series data without partitioning in the S3 bucket. Manually move data that is older than 30 days to separate S3 buckets.
options.B: Create AWS Lambda functions to copy the time-series data into separate S3 buckets. Apply S3 Lifecycle policies to archive data that is older than 30 days to S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval.
options.C: Organize the time-series data into partitions by date prefix in the S3 bucket. Apply S3 Lifecycle policies to archive partitions that are older than 30 days to S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval.
options.D: Put each day's time-series data into its own S3 bucket. Use S3 Lifecycle policies to archive S3 buckets that hold data that is older than 30 days to S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval.

questions.explanation: C. Organize the time-series data into partitions by date prefix in the S3 bucket. Apply S3 Lifecycle policies to archive partitions that are older than 30 days to S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval. Partitioning by date prefix (e.g., s3://bucket/clicks/date=YYYY-MM-DD/) lets Athena scan only the relevant partitions (e.g., last 3 days), drastically improving query performance by avoiding scanning unnecessary data. Athena is optimized for partitioned data and benefits greatly from partition pruning. Applying S3 Lifecycle policies on partitions automates archiving old partitions to Glacier after 30 days, which manages cost without affecting recent data performance.

questions.question: A company has deployed an ML model that detects fraudulent credit card transactions in real time in a banking application. The model uses Amazon SageMaker Asynchronous Inference. Consumers are reporting delays in receiving the inference results. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to improve the inference performance. The solution also must provide a notification when a deviation in model quality occurs. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use SageMaker real-time inference for inference. Use SageMaker Model Monitor for notifications about model quality.
options.B: Use SageMaker batch transform for inference. Use SageMaker Model Monitor for notifications about model quality.
options.C: Use SageMaker Serverless Inference for inference. Use SageMaker Inference Recommender for notifications about model quality.
options.D: Keep using SageMaker Asynchronous Inference for inference. Use SageMaker Inference Recommender for notifications about model quality.

questions.explanation: A. Use SageMaker real-time inference for inference. Use SageMaker Model Monitor for notifications about model quality. Inference performance: SageMaker Asynchronous Inference is good for long-running inference tasks but can introduce delays because it’s a queue-based, decoupled process. For real-time fraud detection, real-time inference provides low-latency, immediate responses, improving user experience. Model quality monitoring: SageMaker Model Monitor is the recommended service for continuous monitoring of deployed model quality, data drift, and bias, with built-in alerts and notifications.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to host a trained ML model. The rate of requests to the model will be inconsistent throughout the day. The ML engineer needs a scalable solution that minimizes costs when the model is not in use. The solution also must maintain the model's capacity to respond to requests during times of peak usage. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create AWS Lambda functions that have fixed concurrency to host the model. Configure the Lambda functions to automatically scale based on the number of requests to the model.
options.B: Deploy the model on an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) cluster that uses AWS Fargate. Set a static number of tasks to handle requests during times of peak usage.
options.C: Deploy the model to an Amazon SageMaker endpoint. Deploy multiple copies of the model to the endpoint. Create an Application Load Balancer to route traffic between the different copies of the model at the endpoint.
options.D: Deploy the model to an Amazon SageMaker endpoint. Create SageMaker endpoint auto scaling policies that are based on Amazon CloudWatch metrics to adjust the number of instances dynamically.

questions.explanation: D. Deploy the model to an Amazon SageMaker endpoint. Create SageMaker endpoint auto scaling policies that are based on Amazon CloudWatch metrics to adjust the number of instances dynamically. SageMaker real-time endpoints support auto scaling, which dynamically adjusts the number of instances based on traffic demand. During low or no traffic periods, the endpoint scales down, minimizing costs. During peak times, it scales up to maintain responsiveness. This approach provides a managed, scalable, and cost-efficient solution tailored for ML model serving with variable request rates.

questions.question: A company uses Amazon SageMaker Studio to develop an ML model. The company has a single SageMaker Studio domain. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution that provides an automated alert when SageMaker compute costs reach a specific threshold. Which solution will meet these requirements?
