options.A: Add resource tagging by editing the SageMaker user profile in the SageMaker domain. Configure AWS Cost Explorer to send an alert when the threshold is reached.
options.B: Add resource tagging by editing the SageMaker user profile in the SageMaker domain. Configure AWS Budgets to send an alert when the threshold is reached.
options.C: Add resource tagging by editing each user's IAM profile. Configure AWS Cost Explorer to send an alert when the threshold is reached.
options.D: Add resource tagging by editing each user's IAM profile. Configure AWS Budgets to send an alert when the threshold is reached.

questions.explanation: B. Add resource tagging by editing the SageMaker user profile in the SageMaker domain. Configure AWS Budgets to send an alert when the threshold is reached. Resource tagging via SageMaker user profiles allows you to tag resources created by each user in SageMaker Studio. Tags enable cost allocation tracking specific to SageMaker Studio users. AWS Budgets is designed to monitor costs and usage and send alerts when thresholds are exceeded. Cost Explorer is mainly used for cost analysis and visualization but does not support automatic alerts like AWS Budgets.

questions.question: A company uses Amazon SageMaker for its ML workloads. The company's ML engineer receives a 50 MB Apache Parquet data file to build a fraud detection model. The file includes several correlated columns that are not required. What should the ML engineer do to drop the unnecessary columns in the file with the LEAST effort?

options.A: Download the file to a local workstation. Perform one-hot encoding by using a custom Python script.
options.B: Create an Apache Spark job that uses a custom processing script on Amazon EMR.
options.C: Create a SageMaker processing job by calling the SageMaker Python SDK.
options.D: Create a data flow in SageMaker Data Wrangler. Configure a transform step.

questions.explanation: D. Create a data flow in SageMaker Data Wrangler. Configure a transform step. SageMaker Data Wrangler provides a low-code, easy-to-use interface for data preparation tasks such as dropping columns, filtering data, and feature engineering. It’s designed to minimize effort and accelerate the data preparation workflow. A transform step in Data Wrangler lets you easily drop unnecessary columns without writing custom code.

questions.question: A company is creating an application that will recommend products for customers to purchase. The application will make API calls to Amazon Q Business. The company must ensure that responses from Amazon Q Business do not include the name of the company's main competitor. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Configure the competitor's name as a blocked phrase in Amazon Q Business.
options.B: Configure an Amazon Q Business retriever to exclude the competitor’s name.
options.C: Configure an Amazon Kendra retriever for Amazon Q Business to build indexes that exclude the competitor's name.
options.D: Configure document attribute boosting in Amazon Q Business to deprioritize the competitor's name.

questions.explanation: A. Configure the competitor's name as a blocked phrase in Amazon Q Business. Amazon Q (Amazon Q Business) allows you to configure blocked phrases to prevent certain words or phrases from appearing in responses. Blocking the competitor’s name ensures it won’t appear in any answers returned by the service. This directly meets the requirement to exclude the competitor’s name from responses.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to use Amazon SageMaker to fine-tune a large language model (LLM) for text summarization. The ML engineer must follow a low-code no-code (LCNC) approach. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use SageMaker Studio to fine-tune an LLM that is deployed on Amazon EC2 instances.
options.B: Use SageMaker Autopilot to fine-tune an LLM that is deployed by a custom API endpoint.
options.C: Use SageMaker Autopilot to fine-tune an LLM that is deployed on Amazon EC2 instances.
options.D: Use SageMaker Autopilot to fine-tune an LLM that is deployed by SageMaker JumpStart.

questions.explanation: D. Use SageMaker Autopilot to fine-tune an LLM that is deployed by SageMaker JumpStart. SageMaker JumpStart offers pre-trained models including LLMs and provides easy, low-code/no-code workflows to fine-tune these models. SageMaker Autopilot is primarily designed for automating tabular ML tasks and is not suited for fine-tuning large language models directly. SageMaker JumpStart supports easy fine-tuning of pre-built LLMs with minimal code, fitting the LCNC requirement.

questions.question: A company has an ML model that needs to run one time each night to predict stock values. The model input is 3 MB of data that is collected during the current day. The model produces the predictions for the next day. The prediction process takes less than 1 minute to finish running. How should the company deploy the model on Amazon SageMaker to meet these requirements?

options.A: Use a multi-model serverless endpoint. Enable caching.
options.B: Use an asynchronous inference endpoint. Set the InitialInstanceCount parameter to 0.
options.C: Use a real-time endpoint. Configure an auto scaling policy to scale the model to 0 when the model is not in use.
options.D: Use a serverless inference endpoint. Set the MaxConcurrency parameter to 1.

questions.explanation: D. Use a serverless inference endpoint. Set the MaxConcurrency parameter to 1. The model runs once per night with a small input size (3 MB) and short execution time (<1 minute). Serverless inference is designed for infrequent, bursty workloads with low latency and no need to manage infrastructure. It automatically scales to zero when not in use, minimizing cost. Setting MaxConcurrency to 1 fits the low concurrency need (one inference job at a time).

questions.question: An ML engineer trained an ML model on Amazon SageMaker to detect automobile accidents from dosed-circuit TV footage. The ML engineer used SageMaker Data Wrangler to create a training dataset of images of accidents and non-accidents. The model performed well during training and validation. However, the model is underperforming in production because of variations in the quality of the images from various cameras. Which solution will improve the model's accuracy in the LEAST amount of time?

options.A: Collect more images from all the cameras. Use Data Wrangler to prepare a new training dataset.
options.B: Recreate the training dataset by using the Data Wrangler corrupt image transform. Specify the impulse noise option.
options.C: Recreate the training dataset by using the Data Wrangler enhance image contrast transform. Specify the Gamma contrast option.
options.D: Recreate the training dataset by using the Data Wrangler resize image transform. Crop all images to the same size.

questions.explanation: C. Recreate the training dataset by using the Data Wrangler enhance image contrast transform. Specify the Gamma contrast option. The issue is variations in image quality from different cameras. Enhancing image contrast helps normalize brightness and contrast differences across images. This transform is quick to apply and can improve the model’s robustness to varying image quality without needing new data collection. Using Data Wrangler makes this low code and fast to implement.

questions.question: A company has an application that uses different APIs to generate embeddings for input text. The company needs to implement a solution to automatically rotate the API tokens every 3 months. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Store the tokens in AWS Secrets Manager. Create an AWS Lambda function to perform the rotation.
options.B: Store the tokens in AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store. Create an AWS Lambda function to perform the rotation.
options.C: Store the tokens in AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use an AWS managed key to perform the rotation.
options.D: Store the tokens in AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use an AWS owned key to perform the rotation.

questions.explanation: A. Store the tokens in AWS Secrets Manager. Create an AWS Lambda function to perform the rotation. AWS Secrets Manager is designed specifically to store secrets like API tokens, credentials, and supports automatic secret rotation via Lambda functions. It can securely store tokens and rotate them automatically on a defined schedule (e.g., every 3 months). Lambda functions can implement custom logic to retrieve new tokens and update the secret.

questions.question: An ML engineer receives datasets that contain missing values, duplicates, and extreme outliers. The ML engineer must consolidate these datasets into a single data frame and must prepare the data for ML. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to import the datasets and to consolidate them into a single data frame. Use the cleansing and enrichment functionalities to prepare the data.
options.B: Use Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth to import the datasets and to consolidate them into a single data frame. Use the human-in-the-loop capability to prepare the data.
options.C: Manually import and merge the datasets. Consolidate the datasets into a single data frame. Use Amazon Q Developer to generate code snippets that will prepare the data.
options.D: Manually import and merge the datasets. Consolidate the datasets into a single data frame. Use Amazon SageMaker data labeling to prepare the data.

questions.explanation: A. Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to import the datasets and to consolidate them into a single data frame. Use the cleansing and enrichment functionalities to prepare the data. SageMaker Data Wrangler is designed to simplify the data preparation process. It allows importing multiple datasets, merging them, and offers built-in capabilities to handle missing values, duplicates, and outliers. It provides a visual interface with data cleansing and transformation features, which greatly reduces manual coding effort and accelerates the preparation phase. It’s ideal for consolidating multiple datasets and preparing them for ML in a streamlined way.

questions.question: A company has historical data that shows whether customers needed long-term support from company staff. The company needs to develop an ML model to predict whether new customers will require long-term support. Which modeling approach should the company use to meet this requirement?

options.A: Anomaly detection
options.B: Linear regression
options.C: Logistic regression
options.D: Semantic segmentation

questions.explanation: C. Logistic regression The problem is to predict a binary outcome: whether new customers will or will not require long-term support. Logistic regression is a standard classification algorithm used to predict a binary target variable. It outputs probabilities that a given input belongs to one of two classes (e.g., long-term support needed: yes/no).

questions.question: An ML engineer has developed a binary classification model outside of Amazon SageMaker. The ML engineer needs to make the model accessible to a SageMaker Canvas user for additional tuning. The model artifacts are stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. The ML engineer and the Canvas user are part of the same SageMaker domain. Which combination of requirements must be met so that the ML engineer can share the model with the Canvas user? (Choose two.)

options.A: The ML engineer and the Canvas user must be in separate SageMaker domains.
options.B: The Canvas user must have permissions to access the S3 bucket where the model artifacts are stored.
options.C: The model must be registered in the SageMaker Model Registry.
options.D: The ML engineer must host the model on AWS Marketplace.
options.E: The ML engineer must deploy the model to a SageMaker endpoint.

questions.explanation: B. The Canvas user must have permissions to access the S3 bucket where the model artifacts are stored. Since the model artifacts are stored in S3, the user who wants to use or tune the model in SageMaker Canvas needs permission to read those artifacts. C. The model must be registered in the SageMaker Model Registry. Registering the model in the Model Registry enables sharing and versioning of models across users in the same SageMaker domain. This is the standard way to share models with SageMaker Canvas users.

questions.question: A company is building a deep learning model on Amazon SageMaker. The company uses a large amount of data as the training dataset. The company needs to optimize the model's hyperparameters to minimize the loss function on the validation dataset. Which hyperparameter tuning strategy will accomplish this goal with the LEAST computation time?

options.A: Hyperband
options.B: Grid search
options.C: Bayesian optimization
options.D: Random search

questions.explanation: Hyperband is an optimization strategy specifically designed to reduce the computational cost of hyperparameter tuning. It works by intelligently allocating resources to different hyperparameter configurations and quickly eliminating underperforming ones.

questions.question: A company is planning to use Amazon Redshift ML in its primary AWS account. The source data is in an Amazon S3 bucket in a secondary account. An ML engineer needs to set up an ML pipeline in the primary account to access the S3 bucket in the secondary account. The solution must not require public IPv4 addresses. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Provision a Redshift cluster and Amazon SageMaker Studio in a VPC with no public access enabled in the primary account. Create a VPC peering connection between the accounts. Update the VPC route tables to remove the route to 0.0.0.0/0.
options.B: Provision a Redshift cluster and Amazon SageMaker Studio in a VPC with no public access enabled in the primary account. Create an AWS Direct Connect connection and a transit gateway. Associate the VPCs from both accounts with the transit gateway. Update the VPC route tables to remove the route to 0.0.0.0/0.
options.C: Provision a Redshift cluster and Amazon SageMaker Studio in a VPC in the primary account. Create an AWS Site-to-Site VPN connection with two encrypted IPsec tunnels between the accounts. Set up interface VPC endpoints for Amazon S3.
options.D: Provision a Redshift cluster and Amazon SageMaker Studio in a VPC in the primary account. Create an S3 gateway endpoint. Update the S3 bucket policy to allow IAM principals from the primary account. Set up interface VPC endpoints for SageMaker and Amazon Redshift.

questions.explanation: Provision a Redshift cluster and Amazon SageMaker Studio in a VPC in the primary account. Create an S3 gateway endpoint. Update the S3 bucket policy to allow IAM principals from the primary account. Set up interface VPC endpoints for SageMaker and Amazon Redshift. Requirement: The solution must not require public IPv4 addresses. Option D provisions Redshift and SageMaker Studio inside a VPC (no public IPs needed). It uses S3 Gateway Endpoints, which enable private, secure access to S3 without traversing the internet. It updates the S3 bucket policy to allow IAM principals from the primary account to access the data. Interface VPC endpoints are set up for SageMaker and Redshift, so traffic stays within the AWS network and does not require public IPs.

questions.question: A company is using an AWS Lambda function to monitor the metrics from an ML model. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to send an email message when the metrics breach a threshold. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Log the metrics from the Lambda function to AWS CloudTrail. Configure a CloudTrail trail to send the email message.
options.B: Log the metrics from the Lambda function to Amazon CloudFront. Configure an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to send the email message.
options.C: Log the metrics from the Lambda function to Amazon CloudWatch. Configure a CloudWatch alarm to send the email message.
options.D: Log the metrics from the Lambda function to Amazon CloudWatch. Configure an Amazon CloudFront rule to send the email message.

questions.explanation: C. Log metrics to Amazon CloudWatch → Configure CloudWatch alarm to send email Lambda functions log custom metrics (or standard metrics) to CloudWatch. CloudWatch alarms can be configured on these metrics. CloudWatch alarms support SNS actions to send email notifications. This is the standard way to send email alerts on metric breaches. Suitable and recommended.

questions.question: A company has used Amazon SageMaker to deploy a predictive ML model in production. The company is using SageMaker Model Monitor on the model. After a model update, an ML engineer notices data quality issues in the Model Monitor checks. What should the ML engineer do to mitigate the data quality issues that Model Monitor has identified?

options.A: Adjust the model's parameters and hyperparameters.
options.B: Initiate a manual Model Monitor job that uses the most recent production data.
options.C: Create a new baseline from the latest dataset. Update Model Monitor to use the new baseline for evaluations.
options.D: Include additional data in the existing training set for the model. Retrain and redeploy the model.

questions.explanation: C .Create a new baseline from the latest dataset. Update Model Monitor to use the new baseline for evaluations. Model Monitor uses a baseline dataset to compare incoming production data against expected data distributions. After a model update, if data quality issues appear, it may be because the baseline no longer reflects the current valid data distribution. Creating a new baseline from the latest dataset (which reflects the updated data distribution) and updating Model Monitor to use this new baseline will help reduce false positives in data quality alerts. This approach addresses data quality issues without immediately retraining or changing the model.

questions.question: A company has an ML model that generates text descriptions based on images that customers upload to the company's website. The images can be up to 50 MB in total size. An ML engineer decides to store the images in an Amazon S3 bucket. The ML engineer must implement a processing solution that can scale to accommodate changes in demand. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Create an Amazon SageMaker batch transform job to process all the images in the S3 bucket.
options.B: Create an Amazon SageMaker Asynchronous Inference endpoint and a scaling policy. Run a script to make an inference request for each image.
options.C: Create an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster that uses Karpenter for auto scaling. Host the model on the EKS cluster. Run a script to make an inference request for each image.
options.D: Create an AWS Batch job that uses an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) cluster. Specify a list of images to process for each AWS Batch job.

questions.explanation: Create an Amazon SageMaker Asynchronous Inference endpoint and a scaling policy. Run a script to make an inference request for each image. Asynchronous inference is designed to handle large payloads (like images) and supports unpredictable workloads. It automatically scales the endpoint based on request load with the scaling policy. Supports high throughput and decouples request submission from processing. This matches well with the requirement to process images up to 50 MB and handle bursts in demand. Operational overhead is low because SageMaker manages the infrastructure and scaling.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to use AWS services to identify and extract meaningful unique keywords from documents. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Use the Natural Language Toolkit (NLTK) library on Amazon EC2 instances for text pre-processing. Use the Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) algorithm to identify and extract relevant keywords.
options.B: Use Amazon SageMaker and the BlazingText algorithm. Apply custom pre-processing steps for stemming and removal of stop words. Calculate term frequency-inverse document frequency (TF-IDF) scores to identify and extract relevant keywords.
options.C: Store the documents in an Amazon S3 bucket. Create AWS Lambda functions to process the documents and to run Python scripts for stemming and removal of stop words. Use bigram and trigram techniques to identify and extract relevant keywords.
options.D: Use Amazon Comprehend custom entity recognition and key phrase extraction to identify and extract relevant keywords.

questions.explanation: D.use Amazon Comprehend custom entity recognition and key phrase extractionto identify and extract relevant keywords. Amazon Comprehend is a fully managed natural language processing (NLP) service that uses machine learning to find insights and relationships in unstructured text. It's designed for developers and data scientists who don't want to build and maintain their own NLP models. Least operational overhead: With Amazon Comprehend, you don't need to provision, manage, or scale any servers. You simply send your documents to the service via an API call, and it returns the results. The service handles all the underlying infrastructure, from the machine learning models to the compute resources. Built-in functionality: The service offers a key phrase extraction API that automatically identifies the main points and most important phrases in a document. For identifying specific, unique keywords that might be domain-specific, you can use custom entity recognition. This feature allows you to train a custom model to identify terms unique to your business or industry without needing deep machine learning expertise.

questions.question: A company needs to give its ML engineers appropriate access to training data. The ML engineers must access training data from only their own business group. The ML engineers must not be allowed to access training data from other business groups. The company uses a single AWS account and stores all the training data in Amazon S3 buckets. All ML model training occurs in Amazon SageMaker. Which solution will provide the ML engineers with the appropriate access?

options.A: Enable S3 bucket versioning.
options.B: Configure S3 Object Lock settings for each user.
options.C: Add cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) policies to the S3 buckets.
options.D: Create IAM policies. Attach the policies to IAM users or IAM roles.

questions.explanation: D. Create IAM policies. Attach the policies to IAM users or IAM roles. The company has multiple business groups and a single AWS account. All training data is stored in S3 buckets. ML engineers should only access data belonging to their own group. This type of access control is best managed via IAM policies that use resource-level permissions. IAM policies can be scoped to specific S3 prefixes or buckets corresponding to each business group. By attaching those policies to IAM users or roles assigned to ML engineers, you enforce least privilege and prevent access to other groups’ data.

questions.question: A company needs to host a custom ML model to perform forecast analysis. The forecast analysis will occur with predictable and sustained load during the same 2-hour period every day. Multiple invocations during the analysis period will require quick responses. The company needs AWS to manage the underlying infrastructure and any auto scaling activities. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Schedule an Amazon SageMaker batch transform job by using AWS Lambda.
options.B: Configure an Auto Scaling group of Amazon EC2 instances to use scheduled scaling.
options.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Serverless Inference with provisioned concurrency.
options.D: Run the model on an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster on Amazon EC2 with pod auto scaling.

questions.explanation: C. Use Amazon SageMaker Serverless Inference with provisioned concurrency. The workload has predictable and sustained load during the same 2-hour period every day. Multiple invocations require quick response times. The company wants AWS to manage infrastructure and auto scaling. SageMaker Serverless Inference automatically manages the compute resources without needing you to provision or manage servers. Provisioned concurrency ensures warm containers are available during the peak period, reducing cold-start latency and enabling fast responses. This approach fits well for predictable daily spikes and offloads infrastructure management to AWS.

questions.question: A company's ML engineer has deployed an ML model for sentiment analysis to an Amazon SageMaker endpoint. The ML engineer needs to explain to company stakeholders how the model makes predictions. Which solution will provide an explanation for the model's predictions?

options.A: Use SageMaker Model Monitor on the deployed model.
options.B: Use SageMaker Clarify on the deployed model.
options.C: Show the distribution of inferences from A/В testing in Amazon CloudWatch.
options.D: Add a shadow endpoint. Analyze prediction differences on samples.

questions.explanation: B. Use SageMaker Clarify on the deployed model. SageMaker Clarify provides explainability and bias detection for ML models. It can explain how the model makes predictions by generating feature importance and SHAP values. It works both during training and on deployed models (post-deployment explainability). This helps stakeholders understand the key factors influencing model predictions.

questions.question: An ML engineer is using Amazon SageMaker to train a deep learning model that requires distributed training. After some training attempts, the ML engineer observes that the instances are not performing as expected. The ML engineer identifies communication overhead between the training instances. What should the ML engineer do to MINIMIZE the communication overhead between the instances?

options.A: Place the instances in the same VPC subnet. Store the data in a different AWS Region from where the instances are deployed.
options.B: Place the instances in the same VPC subnet but in different Availability Zones. Store the data in a different AWS Region from where the instances are deployed.
options.C: Place the instances in the same VPC subnet. Store the data in the same AWS Region and Availability Zone where the instances are deployed.
options.D: Place the instances in the same VPC subnet. Store the data in the same AWS Region but in a different Availability Zone from where the instances are deployed.

questions.explanation: C. Place the instances in the same VPC subnet. Store the data in the same AWS Region and Availability Zone where the instances are deployed. Communication overhead during distributed training is often due to network latency between instances. Placing instances in the same VPC subnet and same Availability Zone (AZ) minimizes network latency and maximizes network throughput between instances. Storing data in the same Region and AZ further reduces latency when accessing the training data. Cross-AZ or cross-region communication adds additional network latency, increasing overhead and slowing distributed training.

questions.question: A company is running ML models on premises by using custom Python scripts and proprietary datasets. The company is using PyTorch. The model building requires unique domain knowledge. The company needs to move the models to AWS. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST effort?

options.A: Use SageMaker built-in algorithms to train the proprietary datasets.
options.B: Use SageMaker script mode and premade images for ML frameworks.
options.C: Build a container on AWS that includes custom packages and a choice of ML frameworks.
options.D: Purchase similar production models through AWS Marketplace.

questions.explanation: B. Use SageMaker script mode and premade images for ML frameworks. The company already has custom Python scripts and is using PyTorch. SageMaker script mode allows you to directly bring your existing training scripts with minimal changes. SageMaker provides premade Docker images for popular ML frameworks like PyTorch, so you don’t need to build and maintain custom containers. This approach minimizes effort because you reuse existing code and leverage managed infrastructure.

questions.question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker and millions of files to train an ML model. Each file is several megabytes in size. The files are stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. The company needs to improve training performance. Which solution will meet these requirements in the LEAST amount of time?

options.A: Transfer the data to a new S3 bucket that provides S3 Express One Zone storage. Adjust the training job to use the new S3 bucket.
options.B: Create an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system. Link the file system to the existing S3 bucket. Adjust the training job to read from the file system.
options.C: Create an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system. Transfer the existing data to the file system. Adjust the training job to read from the file system.
options.D: Create an Amazon ElastiCache (Redis OSS) cluster. Link the Redis OSS cluster to the existing S3 bucket. Stream the data from the Redis OSS cluster directly to the training job.

questions.explanation: B. Create an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system. Link the file system to the existing S3 bucket. Adjust the training job to read from the file system. Amazon FSx for Lustre provides a high-performance, low-latency file system optimized for ML workloads. It can be linked to an S3 bucket so that data is transparently available in the file system without moving data permanently. This significantly improves training performance by providing fast, shared storage with high throughput and low latency. Minimal change to the training job is needed — just change the input data path to FSx for Lustre mount point.

questions.question: A company wants to develop an ML model by using tabular data from its customers. The data contains meaningful ordered features with sensitive information that should not be discarded. An ML engineer must ensure that the sensitive data is masked before another team starts to build the model. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon Made to categorize the sensitive data.
options.B: Prepare the data by using AWS Glue DataBrew.
options.C: Run an AWS Batch job to change the sensitive data to random values.
options.D: Run an Amazon EMR job to change the sensitive data to random values.

questions.explanation: B. Prepare the data by using AWS Glue DataBrew. AWS Glue DataBrew is a visual data preparation tool that lets you clean and normalize data without writing code. DataBrew has built-in transformations including masking sensitive data (e.g., masking, tokenization, hashing) while preserving data structure. It supports tabular data and allows you to mask sensitive columns without discarding them, which meets the requirement. This approach is low code and operationally efficient compared to custom batch jobs.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to deploy ML models to get inferences from large datasets in an asynchronous manner. The ML engineer also needs to implement scheduled monitoring of the data quality of the models. The ML engineer must receive alerts when changes in data quality occur. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Deploy the models by using scheduled AWS Glue jobs. Use Amazon CloudWatch alarms to monitor the data quality and to send alerts.
options.B: Deploy the models by using scheduled AWS Batch jobs. Use AWS CloudTrail to monitor the data quality and to send alerts.
options.C: Deploy the models by using Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) on AWS Fargate. Use Amazon EventBridge to monitor the data quality and to send alerts.
options.D: Deploy the models by using Amazon SageMaker batch transform. Use SageMaker Model Monitor to monitor the data quality and to send alerts.

questions.explanation: D. Deploy the models by using Amazon SageMaker batch transform. Use SageMaker Model Monitor to monitor the data quality and to send alerts. Amazon SageMaker batch transform is designed for asynchronous batch inference on large datasets, which fits the requirement for asynchronous inferences. SageMaker Model Monitor provides built-in capabilities to schedule monitoring jobs to detect data quality issues, model drift, and other metrics. Model Monitor can generate alerts via Amazon CloudWatch alarms, SNS notifications, etc., when data quality changes occur. This solution integrates smoothly and provides a managed, scalable, and operationally efficient approach to both batch inference and monitoring.

questions.question: An ML engineer normalized training data by using min-max normalization in AWS Glue DataBrew. The ML engineer must normalize the production inference data in the same way as the training data before passing the production inference data to the model for predictions. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Apply statistics from a well-known dataset to normalize the production samples.
options.B: Keep the min-max normalization statistics from the training set. Use these values to normalize the production samples.
options.C: Calculate a new set of min-max normalization statistics from a batch of production samples. Use these values to normalize all the production samples.
options.D: Calculate a new set of min-max normalization statistics from each production sample. Use these values to normalize all the production samples.

questions.explanation: B. Keep the min-max normalization statistics from the training set. Use these values to normalize the production samples. To ensure consistency between training and production, the same normalization parameters (such as min and max values) used during training should be applied to production data. Using training set statistics ensures that the model receives data in the exact same scale and distribution as it was trained on, preventing data distribution mismatch. Calculating new normalization statistics from production data (options C and D) may shift the data distribution and cause model performance degradation. Using statistics from a well-known unrelated dataset (option A) is irrelevant to the current model’s training data distribution.

questions.question: A company is planning to use Amazon SageMaker to make classification ratings that are based on images. The company has 6 ТВ of training data that is stored on an Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP system virtual machine (SVM). The SVM is in the same VPC as SageMaker. An ML engineer must make the training data accessible for ML models that are in the SageMaker environment. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Mount the FSx for ONTAP file system as a volume to the SageMaker Instance.
options.B: Create an Amazon S3 bucket. Use Mountpoint for Amazon S3 to link the S3 bucket to the FSx for ONTAP file system.
options.C: Create a catalog connection from SageMaker Data Wrangler to the FSx for ONTAP file system.
options.D: Create a direct connection from SageMaker Data Wrangler to the FSx for ONTAP file system.

questions.explanation: A. Mount the FSx for ONTAP file system as a volume to the SageMaker instance. SageMaker training jobs can access data stored on file systems by mounting them as volumes directly. Since the FSx for NetApp ONTAP file system is in the same VPC as SageMaker, you can mount the FSx ONTAP volumes directly onto the SageMaker training instances. This approach allows SageMaker to access large datasets (multiple terabytes) efficiently without copying data. Options involving SageMaker Data Wrangler (C and D) are not designed to create persistent access or handle large-scale data directly from FSx ONTAP for training jobs. Mountpoint for S3 (option B) links S3 to ONTAP but does not directly provide a method for SageMaker to access the ONTAP data.

questions.question: A company regularly receives new training data from the vendor of an ML model. The vendor delivers cleaned and prepared data to the company's Amazon S3 bucket every 3-4 days. The company has an Amazon SageMaker pipeline to retrain the model. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to run the pipeline when new data is uploaded to the S3 bucket. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational effort?

options.A: Create an S3 Lifecycle rule to transfer the data to the SageMaker training instance and to initiate training.
options.B: Create an AWS Lambda function that scans the S3 bucket. Program the Lambda function to initiate the pipeline when new data is uploaded.
options.C: Create an Amazon EventBridge rule that has an event pattern that matches the S3 upload. Configure the pipeline as the target of the rule.
options.D: Use Amazon Managed Workflows for Apache Airflow (Amazon MWAA) to orchestrate the pipeline when new data is uploaded.

questions.explanation: C. Create an Amazon EventBridge rule that has an event pattern that matches the S3 upload. Configure the pipeline as the target of the rule. EventBridge supports event-driven automation and can trigger workflows based on S3 events such as new object creation. You can set up a rule that listens for new uploads to the specific S3 bucket or prefix and then triggers the SageMaker pipeline directly. This approach is serverless, scalable, and requires minimal operational overhead. It does not require custom code like Lambda scanning or periodic polling. EventBridge integrates natively with SageMaker pipelines to trigger executions.

questions.question: An ML engineer is developing a fraud detection model by using the Amazon SageMaker XGBoost algorithm. The model classifies transactions as either fraudulent or legitimate. During testing, the model excels at identifying fraud in the training dataset. However, the model is inefficient at identifying fraud in new and unseen transactions. What should the ML engineer do to improve the fraud detection for new transactions?

options.A: Increase the learning rate.
options.B: Remove some irrelevant features from the training dataset.
options.C: Increase the value of the max_depth hyperparameter.
options.D: Decrease the value of the max_depth hyperparameter.

questions.explanation: D. Decrease the value of the max_depth hyperparameter. A smaller max_depth reduces the complexity of each tree. This can help prevent the model from memorizing the training data and improve performance on unseen data.

questions.question: A company has a binary classification model in production. An ML engineer needs to develop a new version of the model. The new model version must maximize correct predictions of positive labels and negative labels. The ML engineer must use a metric to recalibrate the model to meet these requirements. Which metric should the ML engineer use for the model recalibration?

options.A: Accuracy
options.B: Precision
options.C: Recall
options.D: Specificity

questions.explanation: A. Accuracy Accuracy measures the proportion of correct predictions (both true positives and true negatives) over all predictions. It balances both positive and negative class predictions.

questions.question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker to create ML models. The company's data scientists need fine-grained control of the ML workflows that they orchestrate. The data scientists also need the ability to visualize SageMaker jobs and workflows as a directed acyclic graph (DAG). The data scientists must keep a running history of model discovery experiments and must establish model governance for auditing and compliance verifications. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use AWS CodePipeline and its integration with SageMaker Studio to manage the entire ML workflows. Use SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking for the running history of experiments and for auditing and compliance verifications.
options.B: Use AWS CodePipeline and its integration with SageMaker Experiments to manage the entire ML workflows. Use SageMaker Experiments for the running history of experiments and for auditing and compliance verifications.
options.C: Use SageMaker Pipelines and its integration with SageMaker Studio to manage the entire ML workflows. Use SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking for the running history of experiments and for auditing and compliance verifications.
options.D: Use SageMaker Pipelines and its integration with SageMaker Experiments to manage the entire ML workflows. Use SageMaker Experiments for the running history of experiments and for auditing and compliance verifications.

questions.explanation: Use SageMaker Pipelines and its integration with SageMaker Studio to manage the entire ML workflows. Use SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking for the running history of experiments and for auditing and compliance verifications. SageMaker Pipelines is designed specifically for ML workflow orchestration, providing DAG visualization and versioning. Integration with Studio lets data scientists work interactively. ML Lineage Tracking provides comprehensive model governance, audit trails, and compliance verification.

questions.question: A company wants to reduce the cost of its containerized ML applications. The applications use ML models that run on Amazon EC2 instances, AWS Lambda functions, and an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) cluster. The EC2 workloads and ECS workloads use Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes to save predictions and artifacts. An ML engineer must identify resources that are being used inefficiently. The ML engineer also must generate recommendations to reduce the cost of these resources. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST development effort?

options.A: Create code to evaluate each instance's memory and compute usage.
options.B: Add cost allocation tags to the resources. Activate the tags in AWS Billing and Cost Management.
options.C: Check AWS CloudTrail event history for the creation of the resources.
options.D: Run AWS Compute Optimizer.

questions.explanation: D .Run AWS Compute Optimizer. AWS Compute Optimizer analyzes your EC2 instances, ECS clusters, Lambda functions, and EBS volumes to provide resource utilization metrics and cost-saving recommendations. It requires no custom code or manual tagging to start getting insights. It helps identify underutilized or oversized resources, which fits the requirement to find inefficient resource usage and provide recommendations. This solution involves minimal development effort, making it the best fit.

questions.question: A company needs to create a central catalog for all the company's ML models. The models are in AWS accounts where the company developed the models initially. The models are hosted in Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) repositories. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Configure ECR cross-account replication for each existing ECR repository. Ensure that each model is visible in each AWS account.
options.B: Create a new AWS account with a new ECR repository as the central catalog. Configure ECR cross-account replication between the initial ECR repositories and the central catalog.
options.C: Use the Amazon SageMaker Model Registry to create a model group for models hosted in Amazon ECR. Create a new AWS account. In the new account, use the SageMaker Model Registry as the central catalog. Attach a cross-account resource policy to each model group in the initial AWS accounts.
options.D: Use an AWS Glue Data Catalog to store the models. Run an AWS Glue crawler to migrate the models from the ECR repositories to the Data Catalog. Configure cross-account access to the Data Catalog.

questions.explanation: Use the Amazon SageMaker Model Registry to create a model group for models hosted in Amazon ECR. Create a new AWS account. In the new account, use the SageMaker Model Registry as the central catalog. Attach a cross-account resource policy to each model group in the initial AWS accounts. The Amazon SageMaker Model Registry is a centralized repository for managing the lifecycle of your machine learning models. It's designed specifically for this purpose, providing a central catalog to organize, track, and manage model versions. This approach offers several key benefits for a multi-account environment:

questions.question: A company has developed a new ML model. The company requires online model validation on 10% of the traffic before the company fully releases the model in production. The company uses an Amazon SageMaker endpoint behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB) to serve the model. Which solution will set up the required online validation with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Use production variants to add the new model to the existing SageMaker endpoint. Set the variant weight to 0.1 for the new model. Monitor the number of invocations by using Amazon CloudWatch.
options.B: Use production variants to add the new model to the existing SageMaker endpoint. Set the variant weight to 1 for the new model. Monitor the number of invocations by using Amazon CloudWatch.
options.C: Create a new SageMaker endpoint. Use production variants to add the new model to the new endpoint. Monitor the number of invocations by using Amazon CloudWatch.
options.D: Configure the ALB to route 10% of the traffic to the new model at the existing SageMaker endpoint. Monitor the number of invocations by using AWS CloudTrail.

questions.explanation: A.Use production variants to add the new model to the existing SageMaker endpoint. Set the variant weight to 0.1 for the new model. Monitor the number of invocations by using Amazon CloudWatch. Amazon SageMaker multi-model endpoints (or endpoints with multiple production variants) allow you to host multiple models on a single endpoint and control the traffic split by assigning weights to each model variant. Setting the variant weight to 0.1 (10%) for the new model means 10% of the traffic will be routed to the new model for online validation, while 90% will continue to be served by the current production model. This approach requires minimal operational overhead because you manage traffic routing within SageMaker without needing to change the ALB configuration or deploy additional infrastructure. You can monitor the invocations via Amazon CloudWatch, which integrates natively with SageMaker endpoints.

questions.question: A company needs to develop an ML model. The model must identify an item in an image and must provide the location of the item. Which Amazon SageMaker algorithm will meet these requirements?

options.A: Image classification
options.B: XGBoost
options.C: Object detection
options.D: K-nearest neighbors (k-NN)

questions.explanation: C. Object detection. Object detection is a computer vision technique that not only identifies the presence of an item in an image but also provides its location. It does this by drawing a "bounding box" around each detected object and assigning a class label to it. This is in contrast to simpler techniques that might just classify the entire image.

questions.question: A company has an Amazon S3 bucket that contains 1 ТВ of files from different sources. The S3 bucket contains the following file types in the same S3 folder: CSV, JSON, XLSX, and Apache Parquet. An ML engineer must implement a solution that uses AWS Glue DataBrew to process the data. The ML engineer also must store the final output in Amazon S3 so that AWS Glue can consume the output in the future. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use DataBrew to process the existing S3 folder. Store the output in Apache Parquet format.
options.B: Use DataBrew to process the existing S3 folder. Store the output in AWS Glue Parquet format.
options.C: Separate the data into a different folder for each file type. Use DataBrew to process each folder individually. Store the output in Apache Parquet format.
options.D: Separate the data into a different folder for each file type. Use DataBrew to process each folder individually. Store the output in AWS Glue Parquet format.

questions.explanation: AWS Glue DataBrew can process various file formats (CSV, JSON, XLSX, Parquet)Since DataBrew can handle datasets with multiple file formats, there is no need to separate files into different folders by type.Apache Parquet is an optimal format for AWS GlueParquet is a columnar format, which is well-suited for AWS Glue and is efficient for later analysis and ML model training."AWS Glue Parquet format" does not existOptions B and D mention "AWS Glue Parquet format," which is incorrect. Parquet is a standard data format and is not exclusive to AWS Glue. Conclusion: Option A is the best solution because it allows DataBrew to process all files in the existing S3 folder and store the output in Apache Parquet format, which is efficient and compatible with AWS Glue.

questions.question: A manufacturing company uses an ML model to determine whether products meet a standard for quality. The model produces an output of "Passed" or "Failed." Robots separate the products into the two categories by using the model to analyze photos on the assembly line. Which metrics should the company use to evaluate the model's performance? (Choose two.)

options.A: Precision and recall
options.B: Root mean square error (RMSE) and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE)
options.C: Accuracy and F1 score
options.D: Bilingual Evaluation Understudy (BLEU) score

questions.explanation: Precision: Measures how many predicted positive cases are actually positive. Important if false positives are costly. Recall: Measures how many actual positive cases are correctly identified. Important if missing positives is costly. Accuracy: Overall proportion of correct predictions (both positives and negatives). F1 Score: Harmonic mean of precision and recall, balances both metrics.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to encrypt all data in transit when an ML training job runs. The ML engineer must ensure that encryption in transit is applied to processes that Amazon SageMaker uses during the training job. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Encrypt communication between nodes for batch processing.
options.B: Encrypt communication between nodes in a training cluster.
options.C: Specify an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key during creation of the training job request.
options.D: Specify an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key during creation of the SageMaker domain.

questions.explanation: B. Encrypt communication between nodes in a training cluster. Encryption in Transit for SageMaker Training Jobs When running a distributed training job on Amazon SageMaker, multiple instances (nodes) work together in a cluster. During this process, data is exchanged between these nodes. To ensure this communication is secure, SageMaker offers an option to encrypt inter-container traffic, which is the data being sent between the containers on different instances.

questions.question: An ML engineer needs to use metrics to assess the quality of a time-series forecasting model. Which metrics apply to this model? (Choose two.)

options.A: Recall
options.B: LogLoss
options.C: Root mean square error (RMSE)
options.D: InferenceLatency

questions.explanation: Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) RMSE is a common metric for evaluating regression models, and it's also a standard for time-series forecasting. It measures the average magnitude of the errors, or the difference between the predicted and actual values. By squaring the errors before averaging them, RMSE gives a higher penalty to large errors, which is often a desirable feature in forecasting where a big miss can be more costly than many small misses. It is a point forecast metric, meaning it evaluates a single-value prediction. Average Weighted Quantile Loss (wQL) Unlike RMSE, which focuses on point forecasts, wQL is a metric for probabilistic forecasting. Probabilistic forecasting provides not just a single predicted value, but a range of possible outcomes at different confidence levels (quantiles). For example, it might predict that demand will be between 100 and 150 units with 80% confidence. wQL measures the accuracy of these quantile forecasts. It is especially useful in business scenarios where the cost of over- and under-predicting is different. For example, a company might want to penalize a model more for under-forecasting demand (stockout) than for over-forecasting (excess inventory). The "weighted" aspect of wQL allows you to adjust this penalty.

questions.question: A company runs Amazon SageMaker ML models that use accelerated instances. The models require real-time responses. Each model has different scaling requirements. The company must not allow a cold start for the models. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create a SageMaker Serverless Inference endpoint for each model. Use provisioned concurrency for the endpoints.
options.B: Create a SageMaker Asynchronous Inference endpoint for each model. Create an auto scaling policy for each endpoint.
options.C: Create a SageMaker endpoint. Create an inference component for each model. In the inference component settings, specify the newly created endpoint. Create an auto scaling policy for each inference component. Set the parameter for the minimum number of copies to at least 1.
options.D: Create an Amazon S3 bucket. Store all the model artifacts in the S3 bucket. Create a SageMaker multi-model endpoint. Point the endpoint to the S3 bucket. Create an auto scaling policy for the endpoint. Set the parameter for the minimum number of copies to at least 1.

questions.explanation: C. Create a SageMaker endpoint. Create an inference component for each model. In the inference component settings, specify the newly created endpoint. Create an auto scaling policy for each inference component. Set the parameter for the minimum number of copies to at least 1. Inference Components and No Cold Start This solution directly addresses all the requirements. Real-time responses and no cold start: By setting the minimum number of copies to at least 1, you ensure that there is always a running instance or a portion of an instance ready to serve predictions. This eliminates the cold start issue, which is crucial for real-time applications. Accelerated instances: SageMaker inference components can be deployed on various instance types, including those with GPUs or other accelerators, to meet the performance needs of deep learning models. Different scaling requirements for each model: Inference components allow you to define a separate auto scaling policy for each model, even if they're hosted on the same endpoint. This means each model can scale independently based on its specific traffic patterns, optimizing resource usage and cost. Meeting all requirements: Inference components are a modern and efficient way to deploy multiple models on a single SageMaker endpoint. They offer granular control over resources and scaling, making them the ideal choice for this scenario.

questions.question: A company uses Amazon SageMaker for its ML process. A compliance audit discovers that an Amazon S3 bucket for training data uses server-side encryption with S3 managed keys (SSE-S3). The company requires customer managed keys. An ML engineer changes the S3 bucket to use server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS). The ML engineer makes no other configuration changes. After the change to the encryption settings, SageMaker training jobs start to fail with AccessDenied errors. What should the ML engineer do to resolve this problem?

options.A: Update the IAM policy that is attached to the execution role for the training jobs. Include the s3:ListBucket and s3:GetObject permissions.
options.B: Update the S3 bucket policy that is attached to the S3 bucket. Set the value of the aws:SecureTransport condition key to True.
options.C: Update the IAM policy that is attached to the execution role for the training jobs. Include the kms:Encrypt and kms:Decrypt permissions.
options.D: Update the IAM policy that is attached to the user that created the training jobs. Include the kms:CreateGrant permission.

questions.explanation: C.update the IAM policy that is attached to the execution role for the training jobs to include the kms:Encrypt and kms:Decrypt permissions. When you enable server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS) on an S3 bucket, you are introducing a new layer of security. SageMaker, when running a training job, needs to access the data in that bucket. The training job's execution role must now have the necessary permissions to interact with the KMS key to both decrypt the data it reads from S3 and encrypt any data it writes back (like model artifacts or checkpoints). kms:Decrypt: This permission is essential for the SageMaker training job to be able to read the encrypted training data from the S3 bucket. Without it, the job will fail with an AccessDenied error because it cannot decrypt the objects. kms:Encrypt: This permission is needed for the SageMaker job to encrypt any output data (e.g., the trained model artifacts) before storing it in the S3 bucket.

questions.question: A company runs training jobs on Amazon SageMaker by using a compute optimized instance. Demand for training runs will remain constant for the next 55 weeks. The instance needs to run for 35 hours each week. The company needs to reduce its model training costs. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use a serverless endpoint with a provisioned concurrency of 35 hours for each week. Run the training on the endpoint.
options.B: Use SageMaker Edge Manager for the training. Specify the instance requirement in the edge device configuration. Run the training.
options.C: Use the heterogeneous cluster feature of SageMaker Training. Configure the instance_type, instance_count, and instance_groups arguments to run training jobs.
options.D: Opt in to a SageMaker Savings Plan with a 1-year term and an All Upfront payment. Run a SageMaker Training job on the instance.

questions.explanation: opt in to a SageMaker Savings Plan with a 1-year term and an All Upfront payment.Run a SageMaker Training job on the instance. A SageMaker Savings Plan is a flexible pricing model that allows you to commit to a consistent amount of usage, measured in dollars per hour, over a one- or three-year term. In exchange for this commitment.

questions.question: A company needs to train an ML model that will use historical transaction data to predict customer behavior.Select the correct AWS service from the following list to perform each task on the data. Each service should be selected one time or not at all. (Select three.) •Amazon Athena•AWS Glue•Amazon Kinesis Data Streams•Amazon S3
questions.explanation: Amazon Athena. Amazon Athena is a serverless interactive query service. It allows you to run SQL queries directly on data stored in Amazon S3. No infrastructure to manage (no servers, clusters, or provisioning). Amazon S3. Amazon S3 is a durable, scalable, and cost-effective object storage service. It is the foundation of most AWS analytics architectures. Supports: Structured data (CSV, Parquet, ORC) Semi-structured data (JSON) Unstructured data Integrates natively with Athena, Glue, Redshift, EMR, and many other services. AWS Glue. AWS Glue is a fully managed ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) service. It: Reads raw data from S3 Cleans, enriches, and transforms it Writes processed data back to S3 Supports: Apache Spark Python and Scala Glue Data Catalog for schema management Common use cases: Data cleansing Format conversion (CSV → Parquet) Aggregations and joins.

questions.question: A company deployed an ML model that uses the XGBoost algorithm to predict product failures. The model is hosted on an Amazon SageMaker endpoint and is trained on normal operating data. An AWS Lambda function provides the predictions to the company's application. An ML engineer must implement a solution that uses incoming live data to detect decreased model accuracy over time. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudWatch to create a dashboard that monitors real-time inference data and model predictions. Use the dashboard to detect drift.
options.B: Modify the Lambda function to calculate model drift by using real-time inference data and model predictions. Program the Lambda function to send alerts.
options.C: Schedule a monitoring job in SageMaker Model Monitor. Use the job to detect drift by analyzing the live data against a baseline of the training data statistics and constraints.
options.D: Schedule a monitoring job in SageMaker Debugger. Use the job to detect drift by analyzing the live data against a baseline of the training data statistics and constraints.

questions.explanation: schedule a monitoring job in SageMaker Model Monitor. Use the job to detect drift by analyzing the live data against a baseline of the training data statistics and constraints. Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor is a fully managed service designed to continuously monitor the quality of ML models in production. It directly addresses the problem of detecting decreased model accuracy over time, a phenomenon known as model drift or concept drift.

questions.question: A company has an ML model that uses historical transaction data to predict customer behavior. An ML engineer is optimizing the model in Amazon SageMaker to enhance the model's predictive accuracy. The ML engineer must examine the input data and the resulting predictions to identify trends that could skew the model's performance across different demographics. Which solution will provide this level of analysis?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudWatch to monitor network metrics and CPU metrics for resource optimization during model training.
options.B: Create AWS Glue DataBrew recipes to correct the data based on statistics from the model output.
options.C: Use SageMaker Clarify to evaluate the model and training data for underlying patterns that might affect accuracy.
options.D: Create AWS Lambda functions to automate data pre-processing and to ensure consistent quality of input data for the model.

questions.explanation: use SageMaker Clarify to evaluate the model and training data for underlying patterns that might affect accuracy. SageMaker Clarify is a dedicated tool within Amazon SageMaker designed to help you detect and mitigate bias and to provide explainability for your machine learning models

questions.question: A company uses 10 Reserved Instances of accelerated instance types to serve the current version of an ML model. An ML engineer needs to deploy a new version of the model to an Amazon SageMaker real-time inference endpoint. The solution must use the original 10 instances to serve both versions of the model. The solution also must include one additional Reserved Instance that is available to use in the deployment process. The transition between versions must occur with no downtime or service interruptions. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Configure a blue/green deployment with all-at-once traffic shifting.
options.B: Configure a blue/green deployment with canary traffic shifting and a size of 10%.
options.C: Configure a shadow test with a traffic sampling percentage of 10%.
options.D: Configure a rolling deployment with a rolling batch size of 1.

questions.explanation: B. Configure a blue/green deployment with canary traffic shifting and a size of 10%. Gradually shifts 10% of traffic to the new model (canary). Allows monitoring and rollback if issues arise. This is a low-risk, best practice approach for model validation.

questions.question: An IoT company uses Amazon SageMaker to train and test an XGBoost model for object detection. ML engineers need to monitor performance metrics when they train the model with variants in hyperparameters. The ML engineers also need to send Short Message Service (SMS) text messages after training is complete. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudWatch to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) for message delivery.
options.B: Use Amazon CloudWatch to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) for message delivery.
options.C: Use AWS CloudTrail to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) for message delivery.
options.D: Use AWS CloudTrail to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) for message delivery.

questions.explanation: B. Use Amazon CloudWatch to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) for message delivery. This combination leverages the strengths of two dedicated AWS services to create a complete monitoring and alerting system. Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and observability service designed to collect metrics, logs, and events from your AWS resources and applications. It's the right choice for monitoring performance because it provides real-time data on things like CPU utilization, network traffic, and application latency. CloudWatch allows you to create alarms that trigger when a specific metric crosses a defined threshold. Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) is a fully managed publish/subscribe messaging service. When a CloudWatch alarm triggers, it can publish a message to an SNS topic. This topic then immediately delivers the message to all of its subscribed endpoints, which can include email addresses, SMS, AWS Lambda functions, and more. This "fan-out" capability makes it ideal for broadcasting real-time notifications to multiple recipients simultaneously.

questions.question: A company is working on an ML project that will include Amazon SageMaker notebook instances. An ML engineer must ensure that the SageMaker notebook instances do not allow root access. Which solution will prevent the deployment of notebook instances that allow root access?

options.A: Use IAM condition keys to stop deployments of SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access.
options.B: Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) keys to stop deployments of SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access.
options.C: Monitor resource creation by using Amazon EventBridge events. Create an AWS Lambda function that deletes all deployed SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access.
options.D: Monitor resource creation by using AWS CloudFormation events. Create an AWS Lambda function that deletes all deployed SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access.

questions.explanation: use IAM condition keys to stop deployments of SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access. AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is the service used to manage permissions and control access to AWS resources. IAM condition keys are a powerful feature that allows you to specify conditions under which a policy statement is in effect. For SageMaker notebook instances, there is a specific condition key you can use to check the RootAccess parameter.

questions.question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker to develop ML models. The company stores sensitive training data in an Amazon S3 bucket. The model training must have network isolation from the internet. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Run the SageMaker training jobs in private subnets. Create a NAT gateway. Route traffic for training through the NAT gateway.
options.B: Run the SageMaker training jobs in private subnets. Create an S3 gateway VPC endpoint. Route traffic for training through the S3 gateway VPC endpoint.
options.C: Run the SageMaker training jobs in public subnets that have an attached security group. In the security group, use inbound rules to limit traffic from the internet. Encrypt SageMaker instance storage by using server- side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS).
options.D: Encrypt traffic to Amazon S3 by using a bucket policy that includes a value of True for the aws:SecureTransport condition key. Use default at-rest encryption for Amazon S3. Encrypt SageMaker instance storage by using server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS).

questions.explanation: run the SageMaker training jobs in private subnets and create an S3 gateway VPC endpoint, routing traffic for training through the S3 gateway VPC endpoint. This solution meets the requirements for network isolation and secure access to sensitive data. Private Subnets: Running the SageMaker training jobs in private subnets ensures that they do not have direct access to the public internet. This is a fundamental step for network isolation, as it keeps your training environment and sensitive data securely within your Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC). S3 Gateway VPC Endpoint: SageMaker needs to access the training data stored in Amazon S3. An S3 gateway VPC endpoint provides a private, secure connection between your VPC and the S3 service. By configuring a gateway endpoint and a route table to use it, all traffic from the private subnet to Amazon S3 stays within the AWS network and does not traverse the public internet. This satisfies the requirement for network isolation while still allowing SageMaker to access the necessary data.

questions.question: A company needs an AWS solution that will automatically create versions of ML models as the models are created. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR)
options.B: Model packages from Amazon SageMaker Marketplace
options.C: Amazon SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking
options.D: Amazon SageMaker Model Registry

questions.explanation: D. Amazon SageMaker Model Registry. The Amazon SageMaker Model Registry is a centralized, managed repository specifically designed for versioning, cataloging, and managing machine learning models. It provides a dedicated system to automatically create a new version of a model each time it is created or updated, fulfilling the core requirement of the prompt.

questions.question: A company needs to use Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to supplement an open source large language model (LLM) that runs on Amazon Bedrock. The company's data for RAG is a set of documents in an Amazon S3 bucket. The documents consist of .csv files and .docx files. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Create a pipeline in Amazon SageMaker Pipelines to generate a new model. Call the new model from Amazon Bedrock to perform RAG queries.
options.B: Convert the data into vectors. Store the data in an Amazon Neptune database. Connect the database to Amazon Bedrock. Call the Amazon Bedrock API to perform RAG queries.
options.C: Fine-tune an existing LLM by using an AutoML job in Amazon SageMaker. Configure the S3 bucket as a data source for the AutoML job. Deploy the LLM to a SageMaker endpoint. Use the endpoint to perform RAG queries.
options.D: Create a knowledge base for Amazon Bedrock. Configure a data source that references the S3 bucket. Use the Amazon Bedrock API to perform RAG queries.

questions.explanation: create a knowledge base for Amazon Bedrock, configure a data source that references the S3 bucket, and use the Amazon Bedrock API to perform RAG queries. Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique used to enhance the responses of a Large Language Model (LLM) by providing it with up-to-date, external information. Instead of relying solely on its pre-trained data, the LLM can "retrieve" relevant information from a knowledge base to generate more accurate and contextual responses. Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases is a fully managed service designed specifically to streamline this process. It provides an out-of-the-box solution for implementing a complete RAG workflow. Least Operational Overhead: This is a key advantage. Amazon Bedrock handles the entire RAG pipeline for you, from data ingestion to retrieval and prompt augmentation. You don't have to manage any of the underlying infrastructure, such as vector databases, data ingestion pipelines, or embedding models. Simple Data Source Integration: You can easily point a Bedrock knowledge base to an S3 bucket. The service automatically handles the rest: it reads the documents, chunks them into smaller pieces, converts the text into vector embeddings, and stores them in a managed vector store. API-driven Queries: Once the knowledge base is set up, you can simply use the Amazon Bedrock API to query the LLM. Bedrock automatically uses the knowledge base to retrieve the most relevant information and augments the prompt before sending it to the foundation model, ensuring the response is grounded in your data.

questions.question: A company plans to deploy an ML model for production inference on an Amazon SageMaker endpoint. The average inference payload size will vary from 100 MB to 300 MB. Inference requests must be processed in 60 minutes or less. Which SageMaker inference option will meet these requirements?

options.A: Serverless inference
options.B: Asynchronous inference
options.C: Real-time inference
options.D: Batch transform

questions.explanation: B. Asynchronous inference. SageMaker Asynchronous Inference is specifically designed for use cases with large payloads (up to 1 GB) and/or long processing times (up to one hour). The company's requirements of a 100-300 MB payload and a 60-minute processing time fit perfectly within these limits. When a request is made, the data is placed into a queue, and the endpoint processes it asynchronously, allowing it to handle large, time-consuming tasks without timing out.

questions.question: An ML engineer notices class imbalance in an image classification training job. What should the ML engineer do to resolve this issue?

options.A: Reduce the size of the dataset.
options.B: Transform some of the images in the dataset.
options.C: Apply random oversampling on the dataset.
options.D: Apply random data splitting on the dataset.

questions.explanation: C. Apply random oversampling on the dataset. Class imbalance occurs when some classes have significantly fewer samples than others, which can lead the model to be biased toward the majority class. Random oversampling duplicates samples from the minority class to balance the dataset, helping the model learn better from underrepresented classes.

questions.question: A company receives daily .csv files about customer interactions with its ML model. The company stores the files in Amazon S3 and uses the files to retrain the model. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to mask credit card numbers in the files before the model is retrained. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST development effort?

options.A: Create a discovery job in Amazon Macie. Configure the job to find and mask sensitive data.
options.B: Create Apache Spark code to run on an AWS Glue job. Use the Sensitive Data Detection functionality in AWS Glue to find and mask sensitive data.
options.C: Create Apache Spark code to run on an AWS Glue job. Program the code to perform a regex operation to find and mask sensitive data.
options.D: Create Apache Spark code to run on an Amazon EC2 instance. Program the code to perform an operation to find and mask sensitive data.

questions.explanation: B. Create Apache Spark code to run on an AWS Glue job. Use the Sensitive Data Detection functionality in AWS Glue to find and mask sensitive data. AWS Glue provides built-in Sensitive Data Detection (using AWS Glue DataBrew or Glue Studio), which can automatically detect and mask sensitive data like credit card numbers with minimal coding. Running this on an AWS Glue job scales well and integrates easily with S3. This avoids custom regex coding and infrastructure management.

questions.question: A medical company is using AWS to build a tool to recommend treatments for patients. The company has obtained health records and self-reported textual information in English from patients. The company needs to use this information to gain insight about the patients. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST development effort?

options.A: Use Amazon SageMaker to build a recurrent neural network (RNN) to summarize the data.
options.B: Use Amazon Comprehend Medical to summarize the data.
options.C: Use Amazon Kendra to create a quick-search tool to query the data.
options.D: Use the Amazon SageMaker Sequence-to-Sequence (seq2seq) algorithm to create a text summary from the data.

questions.explanation: B. Use Amazon Comprehend Medical to summarize the data. The prompt asks for a solution to summarize medical data with the least development effort. Amazon Comprehend Medical is a fully managed, pre-trained natural language processing (NLP) service specifically designed for this exact purpose. No Model Training Required: As a pre-trained service, it's ready to use out of the box. You don't need to build, train, or tune any machine learning models. You simply call the API with your medical text, and the service returns a structured output with extracted information. This eliminates all the effort associated with traditional machine learning workflows. Specialized for Medical Text: Unlike general-purpose NLP tools, Amazon Comprehend Medical is fine-tuned to understand the nuances of clinical language. It can accurately identify entities like medical conditions, medications, dosages, and procedures, which is critical for summarizing medical data effectively. HIPAA Compliant: It is a HIPAA-eligible service, which is a crucial consideration for any solution handling patient and clinical data.

questions.question: A company needs to extract entities from a PDF document to build a classifier model. Which solution will extract and store the entities in the LEAST amount of time?

options.A: Use Amazon Comprehend to extract the entities. Store the output in Amazon S3.
options.B: Use an open source AI optical character recognition (OCR) tool on Amazon SageMaker to extract the entities. Store the output in Amazon S3.
options.C: Use Amazon Textract to extract the entities. Use Amazon Comprehend to convert the entities to text. Store the output in Amazon S3.
options.D: Use Amazon Textract integrated with Amazon Augmented AI (Amazon A2I) to extract the entities. Store the output in Amazon S3.

questions.explanation: Amazon Textract is designed to extract text and structured data (like forms and tables) from PDFs and scanned documents. It effectively performs OCR and extracts key information. Amazon Comprehend can then analyze the extracted text to identify entities (such as names, dates, locations) for classification. Combining Textract + Comprehend provides a fully managed, scalable pipeline with minimal development effort. The output can be easily stored in Amazon S3 for downstream processing or training.

questions.question: A company shares Amazon SageMaker Studio notebooks that are accessible through a VPN. The company must enforce access controls to prevent malicious actors from exploiting presigned URLs to access the notebooks. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Set up Studio client IP validation by using the aws:sourceIp IAM policy condition.
options.B: Set up Studio client VPC validation by using the aws:sourceVpc IAM policy condition.
options.C: Set up Studio client role endpoint validation by using the aws:PrimaryTag IAM policy condition.
